programme.title: Tanked

programme.title: Animal Cops Phoenix
programme.desc: Emergency animal medical technicians McHugh and Meadows rush to the aid of two badly injured dogs. Plus, an exotic turtle proves a reluctant patient

programme.title: Animal Cops Phoenix
programme.desc: While the Arizona Humane Society helps a boxer fight for life, medical technician Traci Pepper takes her home and nurses her back to health

programme.title: Alaska: Homestead Rescue

programme.title: Pit Bulls & Parolees
programme.desc: Two strays lead Lizzy and the Villalobos staff on a wild goose chase. A grandmother comes in looking for another canine companion, but her dog Daisy has other plans

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Behaviourist Jackson Galaxy helps a young man deal with his cat phobia. He also returns to Colorado to keep his favourite kitten from the shelter

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Jackson Galaxy deals with his most vicious feline attack in years, at the claws of troubled cat May. He also helps a nervous wreck of a guardian move her feral cats to Canada

programme.title: Lone Star Law
programme.desc: On the Gulf of Mexico, a routine inspection turns treacherous for Carmen Rickel and the Marine Tactical Operations Group. Kyle Hendley wrangles a rattlesnake

programme.title: Dr Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet
programme.desc: Dr Jeff and veterinary technician Ben head to a remote island in Panama to help with the pet population. A sickly Sphynx kitten needs serious medical attention

programme.title: Treehouse Masters
programme.desc: Pete designs a treehouse fit for the ultimate survivalist. He creates a camouflage structure with a luxurious interior that could pamper any hardened champion

programme.title: Blood Ivory
programme.desc: The rangers locate a suspected elephant-killing operation. However, as the sun rises, their own cover is compromised, resulting in a frantic scramble for weapons

programme.title: Alaska: The Last Frontier
programme.desc: Eve kills chickens with her children to teach them the circle of life. Eivin builds a shelter for his horse, and Atz Sr goes on a solo hunt in the Alaskan wild

programme.title: Alaskan Bush People
programme.desc: A storm puts the Browns' first hauling job in peril, and a deal threatens the freedom of the wolf pack. Meanwhile, Noah introduces Christi to the family

programme.title: Mountain Monsters
programme.sub-title: The Silver Giant of Boone County
programme.desc: The team members look for a bear-like creature known as the Silver Giant in Boone County, and build their largest trap ever. Along the way they also learn about other beasts too

programme.title: Alaska: Homestead Rescue
programme.desc: A family in Idaho suffers from a cabin on the verge of collapse and predator threat to 100 chickens. The Raneys set about making it liveable

programme.title: Sharks of the Shadowlands

programme.title: Animal Cops Houston
programme.desc: A dog suffers a panic attack that causes its body temperature to rise to dangerous levels, and the team rescues cats living in squalor

programme.title: Animal Cops Houston
programme.desc: Boris the boar has been abandoned without food or water in disgusting conditions. The race is on to free him and give him a better life

programme.title: Forest, Field & Sky: Art out of Nature
programme.desc: James Fox journeys through six different landscapes across Britain, meeting artists whose work explores the human relationship with the natural world, from Andy Goldsworthy's stone sculptures to James Turrell's extraordinary Skyspaces

programme.title: Reputations
programme.desc: Profile examining the heroic image of American movie star John Wayne, and why he supported the Vietnam war, yet avoided service during the Second World War, supported the Hollywood blacklist and attacked civil rights groups

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.sub-title: The Golden Age of Coach Travel
programme.desc: The history of the coach, which offered a cheaper alternative to rail travel upon its arrival in the 1950s and gave greater freedom to people previously restricted to their own villages and towns due to limited funds. The documentary looks at the distinctive liveries employed by different transport companies and the activities of passengers, including singing and pub visits, as they travelled to destinations such as Blackpool, Margate and Torquay. Narrated by June Whitfield

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The second round of the student quiz continues, with two more teams of four pitting their wits against one another as they attempt to secure a place in the quarter-finals. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.sub-title: Chessmen v Rugby Boys: Semi-Final Two
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts the second semi-final of the quiz as the Chessmen take on the Rugby Boys, making connections between things that initially do not appear to be linked. One set of clues comprises Carmilla, Neo, Lord Voldemort and Sir Leigh Teabing

programme.title: Art on the BBC
programme.desc: Art historian Rose Balston explores six decades of BBC archive to discover how TV has influenced people's understanding of John Constable. The artist pushed the boundaries of landscape painting, to the extent he was rejected by the establishment. But perhaps just as surprisingly, he poured his love for one woman on to the canvas as no painter of landscapes had done before

programme.title: Art of America
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon explores the role of artists and architects in the development of American cities and culture, as the country sought to step out of Europe's shadow. He travels to Manhattan, Chicago and Massachusetts to examine the work of Norman Rockwell, John Sloan, George Bellows and Louis Sullivan, before charting the links between the Great Depression of the 1920s and 30s and the rise of abstract expressionism. He also visits Jackson Pollock's studio in Long Island and Mark Rothko's Chapel in Houston, Texas

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.desc: The team examines the threat of space weather, meeting some of the dedicated people working to keep the Earth safe from the very worst that the Sun can throw at it. Chris Lintott meets Jim Wild, the president of the Royal Astronomical Society, learn about the Carrington Event of 1859, when a massive solar storm caused aurorae as far south as the Caribbean. Maggie Aderin drops into Warwick University to discover how Dr Ravindra Desai's research is helping people grasp how the matter spat out by the sun interacts with the Earth

programme.title: American Visions
programme.desc: Exploration of the cultural impact of the USA's spectacular landscape, revealing how although the country had no ruins or ancient monuments, natural wonders could be found almost anywhere. Presenter Robert Hughes shows how the rise of the railroad opened up a whole new world of discovery while at the same time inaugurating America's industrial future

programme.title: Vienna: Empire, Dynasty and Dream
programme.desc: Historian Simon Sebag Montefiore describes how the Habsburgs transformed Vienna into a multinational city of music, culture, art and ideas. Simon explores the Habsburgs' rise to power and discovers more about how Vienna marked Europe's front line in the struggle to defend both Christendom and the Catholic Church from revolutionaries who plotted to destroy them

programme.title: Muhammad Ali
programme.sub-title: Round Four: The Spell Remains - Part Two
programme.desc: The conclusion of the documentary about the boxer and activist reveals how he retired from fighting in 1981 at the age of 39, before being diagnosed with Parkinson's in 1984. The programme examines how he continued to travel the world spreading his Islamic faith, becoming a symbol of peace and hope, as well as his final two fights and his famous lighting of the Olympic torch at the 1996 Olympics

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.sub-title: Chessmen v Rugby Boys: Semi-Final Two
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts the second semi-final of the quiz as the Chessmen take on the Rugby Boys, making connections between things that initially do not appear to be linked. One set of clues comprises Carmilla, Neo, Lord Voldemort and Sir Leigh Teabing

programme.title: Art of America
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon explores the role of artists and architects in the development of American cities and culture, as the country sought to step out of Europe's shadow. He travels to Manhattan, Chicago and Massachusetts to examine the work of Norman Rockwell, John Sloan, George Bellows and Louis Sullivan, before charting the links between the Great Depression of the 1920s and 30s and the rise of abstract expressionism. He also visits Jackson Pollock's studio in Long Island and Mark Rothko's Chapel in Houston, Texas

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The second round of the student quiz continues, with two more teams of four pitting their wits against one another as they attempt to secure a place in the quarter-finals. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.sub-title: Chessmen v Mathematicians: The Competition for Third Place
programme.desc: Three chess players challenge the Mathematicians in a bid to win third place in the quiz show of patience and lateral thinking, where successful contestants make connections between things that initially do not appear to be linked. Victoria Coren Mitchell presents

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth is waiting for a new three-piece suite to be delivered, boasting it is an exact replica of the one at Sandringham House - and goes to extraordinary lengths to ensure her neighbours see the royal warrant on the delivery van. But, as ever, her family contrives to take the gloss off her moment of glory. Comedy, starring Patricia Routledge

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth decides to take Daddy out for a picnic, but her plans soon backfire. Suburban comedy, starring Patricia Routledge, Clive Swift and Geoffrey Hughes

programme.title: A History of Britain by Simon Schama
programme.desc: Simon Schama investigates how the noble ideals behind the British Empire failed to match the reality. While British society believed it was delivering the benefits of modern Western civilisation, in truth the inhabitants of India and Ireland suffered privation due to a laissez-faire economic policy. Mutiny, famine and lack of religious sensitivity drove a wedge between Britons and Indians, while the great potato famines of the 1840s caused mass migration from Ireland to America, Canada, Australia and New Zealand

programme.title: Muhammad Ali
programme.sub-title: Round Three: The Rivalry - Part One
programme.desc: A look at how the boxer faced his fiercest rival Joe Frazier in the 'Fight of the Century' as he attempted to come back from exile and regain his heavyweight title. Ali also became a focal point for Americans who were growing increasingly disillusioned with the war in Vietnam

programme.title: Muhammad Ali
programme.sub-title: Round Three: The Rivalry - Part Two
programme.desc: The Supreme Court overturns the boxer's conviction, validating his refusal to serve in the war in Vietnam. In 1974 he takes on his greatest rival Joe Frazier in a highly anticipated rematch, but to complete his comeback he must face the fearsome George Foreman

programme.title: Muhammad Ali
programme.sub-title: Round Four: The Spell Remains - Part One
programme.desc: The boxer shocks the world when he defeats George Foreman in the Rumble in the Jungle in Zaire, winning back the heavyweight title and becoming one of the most famous men on Earth. He then faces Joe Frazier for a third time in the brutal Thrilla in Manila

programme.title: Ken Loach Remembers - Up the Junction
programme.desc: Ken Loach on Up the Junction, his landmark 1965 contribution to the BBC's The Wednesday Play strand, adapted from Nell Dunn's celebrated stories of working-class life in Battersea. Blending drama with documentary realism, the production shocked audiences with its frank depiction of sex, illegal abortion and everyday poverty, attracting record complaints while redefining television drama in Britain

programme.title: Up the Junction: The Wednesday Play
programme.desc: A well-to-do girl leaves her parents and comfortable home in fashionable Chelsea to sample life in working-class Battersea, but is not prepared for the culture shock awaiting her. Drama, starring Carol White, Geraldine Sherman and Vickery Turner. Directed by Ken Loach

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.desc: The team examines the threat of space weather, meeting some of the dedicated people working to keep the Earth safe from the very worst that the Sun can throw at it. Chris Lintott meets Jim Wild, the president of the Royal Astronomical Society, learn about the Carrington Event of 1859, when a massive solar storm caused aurorae as far south as the Caribbean. Maggie Aderin drops into Warwick University to discover how Dr Ravindra Desai's research is helping people grasp how the matter spat out by the sun interacts with the Earth

programme.desc: Three chess players challenge the Mathematicians in a bid to win third place in the quiz show of patience and lateral thinking, where successful contestants make connections between things that initially do not appear to be linked. Victoria Coren Mitchell presents

programme.desc: Art historian Rose Balston explores six decades of BBC archive to discover how TV has influenced people's understanding of John Constable. The artist pushed the boundaries of landscape painting, to the extent he was rejected by the establishment. But perhaps just as surprisingly, he poured his love for one woman on to the canvas as no painter of landscapes had done before

programme.title: Reported Missing
programme.desc: Police in Edinburgh receive a call from the university reporting that the family of 19-year-old international student Xiaoqiang have been unable to reach him over the past five days and are becoming increasingly worried about him. A call to Xiaoqiang's mother in China soon reveals why she is so worried, as they are a wealthy family and her son has been asking her for unusually large sums of money. In Glasgow, another woman is worried that her son may be in danger as 20-year-old Daniel has failed to return after leaving to go hiking in the mountains, and it's now nearly nightfall

programme.desc: A look ahead to the latest forecast

programme.title: Doorbell Detectives
programme.desc: Matt Allwright hears from a couple whose shop was robbed not once but twice, taking half-a-million pounds' worth of goods in minutes. Plus, a report on car cannibalism, where thieves strip cars for parts

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Jacqui Joseph wants to solve the mystery of a house in Corby, Dion Dublin seeks enlightenment in a north Wales property and Martin Roberts meets a driving instructor and his wife who are determined to transform a Derby bungalow

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: David Harper presents from one of Britain's biggest antiques fairs in Detling, and brings along a Victorian mystery object. Mark Stacey and Kate Bliss assist the teams

programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: BBC News at One; Weather
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Policing Paradise
programme.desc: Documentary following police officers in Bermuda. A vulnerable elderly man sparks an island-wide search for answers, a roadside stop uncovers a lethal switchblade, and airport officers track suspicious luggage.

programme.title: Robson Green's Weekend Escapes
programme.desc: Actor Mark Benton joins Robson on a Scottish road trip in East Lothian. Robson introduces his friend to bird watching in Musselburgh and books them into a cookery school in Longniddry to prepare lobster - where Mark is dazzled by a baked potato. The duo stay over in a converted double decker bus, before attempting a scaled-down Highland Games, and welly-tossing antics ensue

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Briony May Willams helps an air ambulance pilot to find a rural home that is within an hour of Belfast airport, and also has a go at Irish dancing by Lough Neagh

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Chris Hull and Lee Burkhill travel to Coventry to help mother-and-daughter nurses Barbara and Tate transform their completely empty garden into an exotic jungle retreat

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Family of four Angela, Lianne, Rachel and Nichola compete for cash by crossing a bridge of stepping stones - making sure they avoid the lies. Game show, hosted by Ross Kemp

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Ria Lina joins Alexander Armstrong for the quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers

programme.title: BBC News at Six; Weather
programme.desc: The latest headlines

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Roman Kemp present the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: George spars in the boxing gym but things take a turn, Ian struggles with recent events, and Denise is urged to start treatment

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham and Michaela Strachan reveal the dramatic moments captured over the weekend by the cameras at National Trust Crom in Co Fermanagh

programme.title: Gareth Southgate: Changing the Game for Young Men
programme.desc: Former England men's football manager Gareth Southgate reveals the struggles many boys and young men face, asking why some feel left behind and what could help them move forward. Travelling across the UK, he has honest conversations with young men about how they are feeling and what is really going on in their lives, exploring in detail what is shaping the attitudes and outlook of this generation. His journey takes him to Middlesbrough, where he first made his name in management, to meet young men struggling with unemployment; to a secondary school in Essex, to discover how boys are coping with the demands of the current education system; and to a prison, where he speaks with young inmates to explore what really led them into crime

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: A round-up of today's headlines

programme.title: Pitch Invasion: How the USA Changed Soccer
programme.desc: How superstar players such as Pelé and George Best made America fall in love with soccer in the 1970s, as an entertaining new game emerged, in contrast to English football, which was associated with crumbling stadiums, hooliganism and a grim atmosphere. The New York Cosmos signed Pelé on what was then the biggest contract in sporting history, while Best saw the USA as a chance to reinvent himself and the game. Riding this wave, the NASL boldly predicted global dominance by 1985 - but sadly it was not to be

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: In-depth current affairs report covering a story behind the headlines

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Strictly dancer Amy Dowden turns detective, travelling to west Wales to investigate a rumour of murder in her family. She is shocked to discover that an ancestor died when she was just 14 and investigates how it happened on a farm in 1888. On her dad's side, Amy wants to uncover what led to her grandfather being the only one adopted out of six children. The cause of death of her great grandmother also comes as a painful shock to Amy and she learns about a tale of bravery and hardship in a working-class mining family

programme.desc: Matt Allwright hears from a Doorbell Detective who was robbed in the night by two men while she was asleep upstairs. Plus, a teenage hero whose camera captured the moment he rescued an elderly lady from a burning building

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts sees the warning signs directly spelt out to him at a Merseyside property. Jacqui Joseph meets four friends in Suffolk embarking on their first property project. And Dion Dublin is in Yorkshire where he wants to fill a void

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp is in her home city of Glasgow, where she visits the world-famous Burrell Collection - 9,000 rare antiques and artworks that were donated to the city in 1944. Danny Sebastian and Roo Irvine help the teams

programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Policing Paradise
programme.desc: The drugs unit arrest a trafficker on a cruise ship linked to the deadly drug carfentanyl in a major bust. Officers respond when a woman reports her phone has been stolen by her estranged boyfriend at a luxury golf course, and the police are on hand to assist at a tourist bike crash

programme.title: Robson Green's Weekend Escapes
programme.desc: Robson joins up with Sheli McCoy, AKA Gladiator Sabre, to explore her home area of Fife. A paddleboard tour of the coast is followed by a farm stay overnight. The morning sees them embark on a road trip to Anstruther, where they take a boat trip to the nature reserve of the Isle of May. Famous for its bird life, fauna and flora, the pair attempt to capture the scene with landscape artist Liz Myhill

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps Lisa swap Northampton for a Powys life reboot with her children and takes to a canoe, 128 feet above the ground

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Lee Burkhill and Flo Headlam head to Winchester, Hampshire, to transform Stefan and Lucinda's unloved plot into a garden inspired by their love of making meals outdoors. The pair want to see a 'kitchen' in the garden and a spot to grow produce that they can cook on a barbecue. All this needs to be achieved on a £6,000 budget

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Friends Phoebe, Katie, Sam and Cal aim to win big by stepping in the right direction, avoiding the wrong answers and making it across the bridge to the other side. Game show, hosted by Ross Kemp

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Ria Lina on the quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions

programme.title: BBC News at Six; Weather
programme.desc: The latest headlines

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Roman Kemp present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Kat plans a birthday dinner for Jasmine, Oscar wrestles with the feelings he has for both Josh and Jasmine, and Jack tries to get through to Denise

programme.title: Interior Design Masters with Alan Carr
programme.desc: The two finalists head to Longleat Estate to transform rental cottages, taking inspiration from either the nearby safari park or from the surrounding countryside. They aim to show judges Michelle Ogundehin and Laurence Llewelyn-Bowen how much their design ability has evolved during the competition before one of them wins Interior Design Masters and a partnership with retailer Next for their own homewares range

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Actor and TV presenter Joe Swash traces the history of his father's family, who he lost at the age of 12, knowing only that they supposedly came from Italy. Using documents given to him by his mother and the help of experts, he discovers stories of murder, banditry, a political activist and a family bar in southern Italy

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: A round-up of today's headlines

programme.title: Spotlight
programme.desc: Investigations into social and political issues affecting Northern Ireland

programme.title: England 2006: The Golden Generation
programme.desc: Documentary exploring why the men's England football team did not fulfil their potential at the 2006 FIFA World Cup. Sven-Goran Eriksson's talented side was captained by the talismanic David Beckham, but the players and their partners came under increasing scrutiny off the pitch, and they were eventually eliminated by Cristiano Ronaldo's Portugal. With contributions from their key members of the squad, including Rio Ferdinand, Wayne Rooney, Ashley Cole, Owen Hargreaves and Paul Robinson

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: Pip, Ravi and Connor form a plan that might finally bring their hunt for Jamie to an end, but first Pip must swallow her pride and ask Cara for help. In a daring breaking-and-entering mission, Pip faces the person behind Jamie's disappearance and when a complicated truth emerges, she's forced to accept the line between good an evil is a murky one

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Ibiza: Secrets of the Party Island
programme.desc: Zara McDermott meets Spain's top lawyer when it comes to defending drug traffickers, Ricardo Alvarez-Ossorio, and joins the Spanish Civil Guard as they tackle organised crime and drug trafficking across the country. While on a night shift with an ambulance crew, Zara speaks to a nurse who explains how Ibiza is the ideal testing ground for new drugs

programme.title: High: Confessions of an Ibiza Drug Mule
programme.desc: Michaella discusses how she claimed to have been forced into smuggling in the hope of getting a reduced sentence, only to find herself spending the first of what would be many days behind bars in the cells of the police's anti-narcotics division. However, her story soon caught the attention of the international press, with reporters around the world desperate for the inside scoop

programme.title: Just Act Normal
programme.desc: The siblings navigate life with Dr Feelgood while dodging Leo's suspicions. Jamie takes Tiana on an impromptu date and Ms Jenkins grows concerned about Tanika's home life

programme.title: Just Act Normal
programme.desc: The siblings hire a fake Jackie to fool Ms Jenkins and Tiana clashes with Shanice while juggling work, studies and family. Dr Feelgood tries to leave drug dealing behind

programme.title: Sunny D
programme.desc: Comedy, written by and starring Dane Baptiste as a frustrated 29-year-old, still living at home with his parents. Dane dreads a party to celebrate his dad's 65th birthday, knowing his relatives are going to have awkward questions about his career and love life. When he tries to boost his confidence with the aid of a bottle of rum, it leads to some awkward revelations and an accidental proposal

programme.title: Sunny D
programme.desc: Dane decides to move out of his parents' house and get a place with Nicola. The problem is that his boss Kerry is determined to seduce him, and with so much at stake he cannot afford to get on the wrong side of her. He unwisely encourages her advances, only to find himself in a compromising position that could ruin all his plans. Katherine Ryan guest stars

programme.title: Sunny D
programme.desc: Dane dreads having to introduce Nicola's father, who despises him, to his own parents. The meeting is made even more stressful when it falls on the same day as a work football match, overseen by Kerry. Fortunately, Kadean is on hand to interrupt the tension in the family get-together with a shocking revelation

programme.title: Sunny D
programme.desc: Dane's life has finally hit rock bottom, as Nicola refuses to speak to him following an incident involving her father, while Kerry continues to make his life a misery at work. Some words of advice from one of his heroes give him the confidence to try and put things right - while Stefan comes up with a plan to solve all his problems

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's news

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall dives into the world of the caiman, learns about the plight of the world's biggest parrot and tries to find the giant river otter

programme.title: Live International Football
programme.desc: France v Northern Ireland (Kick-off 8.10pm). Stephen Watson presents all the action from the friendly at Decathlon Arena - Stade Pierre-Mauroy in Lille. This is the French's final warm-up match before they get their World Cup campaign under way against Senegal in New Jersey on June 16, and they will go into the tournament expected to be among the leading contenders once again after finishing as runners-up to Argentina four years ago and lifting the trophy in 2018. Northern Ireland failed to qualify for the finals, but head coach Michael O'Neill will see this as an excellent opportunity to assess how his side measures up against one of international football's elite teams

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May each buy a rear-wheel-drive coupe for £1,500 and find themselves entered into a French ice race. Richard discovers a way to fill the waiting time for flights by inventing the sport of airport-vehicle racing, Jeremy gets behind the wheel of the BMW Z4, and actress Sienna Miller is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Ibiza: Secrets of the Party Island
programme.desc: Zara McDermott delves into the dark economy that keeps the drugs flowing in Ibiza, meeting with the drug supplier who never deals on the streets but feeds a seemingly never-ending supply of Class A drugs to demanding clientele all over the island. She also speaks to the police officers on the front line who are trying to cope with the excessive drug use, often allowing users with small supplies to walk away in order to target the dealers in the act of selling. Officers explain how social media has made their jobs much more difficult and Zara follows as they raid a luxury car rental company next to the main airport

programme.title: Charlie Cooper's Myth Country
programme.desc: The writer and actor heads to Wiltshire, home to ancient sites such as Stonehenge, Avebury and the Westbury chalk horse, to get to the bottom of why humans feel the need to leave marks on the landscape. He turns his investigation towards the far more recent phenomenon of crop circles, hoping to settle a debate that has plagued his family for years. They think that crop circles are the work of aliens, but Charlie strongly doubts that belief

programme.title: Charlie Cooper's Myth Country
programme.desc: The actor travels to Wales, the land of dragons and song, on a mission to discover if King Arthur was a real man or just a myth and if there's any truth behind this treasure story. In the process, he meets treasure hunters of different sorts, a recorder of rare and ancient folk songs, and finds what he refers to as 'his tribe', a metal-detecting club from south Wales. He's optimistic he will return home a rich man - if not in wealth, then certainly in knowledge

programme.title: High: Confessions of an Ibiza Drug Mule
programme.desc: Michaella explains she was faced with a dilemma - stick to her story about being forced to carry the drugs and face 15 years in jail, or come clean and break her mother's heart

programme.title: High: Confessions of an Ibiza Drug Mule
programme.desc: Despite being sentenced to nearly seven years in a maximum security prison, Michaella was home in less than three. She reveals how she got out early and how the experience changed her for the better

programme.title: Brickies
programme.desc: Molly realises her career aspirations will only be reached if she can overcome her fear of driving, and the brickies have a heavy night out watching England v Germany in Euro 2020

programme.title: Brickies
programme.desc: The builders head to a village in Derbyshire, where a retaining wall is needed to protect a housing development from possible land slips and flooding. Multi-talented Lauren is drafted in to help give things a push, but she is not impressed by some of the attitudes on site. Lucas experiences setbacks on and off site as he confesses that his relationship is on the rocks. After struggling to get to grips with a new method of bricklaying, he pulls on the boxing gloves and enjoys sparring with Lauren

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Animated short film about an old-fashioned video-game shop owner in 1995 who faces competition from a slick rival with the latest console in stock. With the voices of Emily Atack and Jamie Demetriou

programme.desc: A round-up of the day's news

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall heads to a place very few people ever go, Snake Island in Brazil, where the concentration of snakes is higher than anywhere else on earth

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall heads to Slovenia in search of the nose-horned viper and also hopes to track down a bear and goes deep into a cave system to find a dragon

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The three hosts are each given a budget of £5,000 to find a car that can hold its own on a racetrack and handle everyday tasks, before testing their purchases in Germany. James May puts two Porsches through their paces on the Top Gear test circuit, and former Downing Street communications director Alastair Campbell tries out the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Louis Theroux: My Scientology Movie (2015)
programme.desc: The reporter investigates the controversial religious organisation, but finds himself denied access to anyone involved. Adopting a different approach, he resorts to recruiting actors to re-enact the personal stories of former members - only to find his project is under surveillance by representatives of the church

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The presenters search for the world's best four-seat supercar, pitting the Aston Martin Rapide against the Porsche Panamera Turbo and Maserati Quattroporte in a series of unusual challenges in the south of England. Harry Potter star Rupert Grint and ex-Formula One driver Rubens Barrichello each do a lap in the Reasonably Priced Car and Richard Hammond puts the Chevrolet Camaro SS and Mercedes E63 AMG through their paces on the test track

programme.title: Charlie Cooper's Myth Country
programme.desc: The writer and actor tours the UK in his campervan, exploring local legends, myths and folklore and gathering evidence in a bid to get to the truth. He begins by going in search of Black Shuck, the demon dog of East Anglia, attempting an overnight stakeout to capture it on film, with unexpected consequences

programme.title: Ladhood
programme.desc: Liam examines his relationship with drugs after he finds himself doing cocaine at a warehouse party with a stranger on a Tuesday night, when he should be preparing for his mental health assessment the next day

programme.title: Ladhood
programme.desc: Liam lies to Jess about missing his mental-health assessment and tensions in their relationship reach breaking point, leaving him suddenly nostalgic for the simplicity of first love

programme.title: Peacock
programme.desc: Liz's disappearance has left an empty space at the gym which Andy and Jay compete to fill - and with his body positivity classes struggling and his relationship drifting, Andy cannot afford to lose. Comedy, starring Allan Mustafa

programme.title: Peacock
programme.desc: Andy starts his new job at a different gym but is soon spooked by high-energy fitness influencer Kara's talk of high standards. What will become of his long-distance bromance with Spooner?

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: A short comedy film about sleep deprivation, the brutality of the music industry and the power of Iggy Pop's hip thrust

programme.title: Louis Theroux: My Scientology Movie (2015)
programme.desc: The reporter investigates the controversial religious organisation, but finds himself denied access to anyone involved. Adopting a different approach, he resorts to recruiting actors to re-enact the personal stories of former members - only to find his project is under surveillance by representatives of the church

programme.title: If Beale Street Could Talk (2018)
programme.desc: In early 1970s Harlem, 19-year old Tish Rivers recalls the longtime relationship between her and fiancé Alonzo 'Fonny' Hunt. With a child on the way, their plans of starting a family and embracing the future are derailed when Fonny is arrested for a crime he did not commit. Director Barry Jenkins' Oscar-winning drama, starring KiKi Layne and Stephan James

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Bob and Paul travel to Dorset to try to catch grayling in the River Frome, focusing on a stretch of water where the British record for the fish was set, so hopes are high that they could catch something big Later, they attempt mackerel fishing on Chesil Beach, close to the feeding peak at dusk

programme.title: Expedition with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve and the team head to the Tian Shan mountains of Kyrgyzstan in search of snow leopards in the remote Djangart valley Joining forces with Kyrgyz conservation group Snow Leopard Foundation, Steve and the team set out to find evidence of a breeding population of snow leopards in the hope of persuading the government of Kyrgyzstan to turn the valley into a protected nature reserve

programme.title: Half Man
programme.desc: Niall has managed to build a new life, only for old ties to resurface when Mona reconnects with Ruben. Before long events begin to spiral toward an intense and devastating conclusion. Hard-hitting drama, written by and starring Richard Gadd, with Jamie Bell, Amy Manson and Neve McIntosh

programme.title: Dear England
programme.desc: As Euro 2024 begins, England arrive as favourites for Southgate's final tournament. Gareth is forced to make some challenging changes to the team and puts his trust in a new generation. When the team struggles unexpectedly and pressure mounts, Gareth wonders how his story will end

programme.title: Greatest Gardens with Diarmuid Gavin and Carol Klein
programme.desc: The search for Northern Ireland's greatest garden is almost at an end - five passionate gardeners have reached the final. After an early autumn road trip spent revisiting the five incredible gardens, Carol and Diarmuid join the finalists, their families and friends at Greenmount's walled garden in Co Antrim. The pair reveal which one of the five will be named the winner

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: Clive Myrie looks at the health benefits of press-ups. He meets Cardiff business owner Shalize, who is concerned about her family's history of type 2 diabetes. Clive suggests that if she does press-ups on a regular basis, the science shows they could reduce the risk of diabetes, lower blood pressure and even help her live longer

This event returned after a 52-year absence in 2025, and it was won by qualifier and 2022 Wimbledon semi-finalist Tatjana Maria, who defeated Leylah Fernandez in the opening round and went on to defeat eighth seed Amanda Anisimova in the showpiece match

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo begins a journey across the Balkan countries of Croatia and Bosnia and Herzegovina. His first leg takes him from Pula, on Croatia's Istrian peninsula, to the town of Buzet. Along the way he takes up shield and sword in a 2,000-year-old Roman amphitheatre, explores an organic olive farm at Chiavalon and rides in the presidential Cadillac to the summer residence of Marshal Josip Tito, the communist president of Yugoslavia until his death in 1980

programme.title: Tartan Tales
programme.desc: A behind-the-scenes look at the kilt shop on Sandy Row, the only one of its kind on the island of Ireland, where history, identity, community and family come together. In a hectic time for the shop and its staff, they prepare for an Ulster-Scots wedding where different family tartans take pride of place in a celebration to honour tradition and celebrate love

Northern Ireland failed to qualify for the finals, but head coach Michael O'Neill will see this as an excellent opportunity to assess how his side measures up against one of international football's elite teams

programme.title: Chronicles of the Sperrins

programme.title: Weather

programme.title: Brexit: A Very British Civil War
programme.desc: The events surrounding the referendum to leave the EU, beginning as David Cameron won a shock majority at the 2015 general election, with the implications immediately dawning on all concerned. There is no coalition negotiation to stop the Conservatives from fulfilling an election pledge to hold a referendum on whether to leave the EU. Nigel Farage resigns as UKIP leader, but quickly U-turns and sets up Leave EU to campaign for Brexit, while Conservative MPs Boris Johnson and Michael Gove decide whether to back Leave or Remain

programme.title: Ali & Ava (2021)
programme.desc: Devoted mother and grandmother Ava, a teaching assistant with a love of country and folk music, is offered a lift home from school by the charismatic Ali, who is estranged from his wife but continuing with the charade of marital domesticity for the sake of their close-knit relatives in Bradford. On the journey, they find many things in common, including a love of music, and begin an unusual and secretive courtship. Romantic drama, starring Adeel Akhtar and Claire Rushbrook

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Adam Henson are in Lisburn, Northern Ireland, for the 157th Balmoral Show, the region's largest agricultural and food event, welcoming more than 100,000 visitors each year. Adam explores heritage crafts that the community are determined to preserve and joins families showing their prize livestock, while Sean is in charge of the stopwatch at the Tractor handling competition

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham, Michaela Strachan and the team continue to broadcast live from the National Trust's Crom Estate as nature writes its own stories. The live remote cameras are capturing fledging, predation and drama around the clock, from otters on the lough shore to red squirrels in ancient woodlands. Iolo Williams takes a look at the lives of some of the country's rarest species at the Knepp rewilding estate in Sussex

programme.title: Cher Meets Rylan
programme.desc: The singer and actress chats to the Radio 2 presenter about her album Christmas, her biggest hits and her incredible life and career. Throughout the show there is archive footage from across the decades, illustrating Cher's glittering music career, her success in film, the many memorable fashion choices, and how she has influenced so many

programme.desc: Chris Hull and Diarmuid Gavin are in Oxford to help Adam, Vaneesha and her father, Graham, transform their two triangular, mirror-image gardens each with a patio and a solitary shed into a family-friendly space, with a budget of £6,000

programme.title: Farm 999
programme.sub-title: Badger Baiters Tracked and Race Against Wildfire
programme.desc: In Northern Ireland, a devastating wildfire tears through the Glens of Antrim, threatening farmland, homes and livestock. As flames spread across the hills, the fire service battle to save buildings, animals and livelihoods. With a drone team and the Irish Air Corps called in, it becomes a race against time to stop the blaze before it consumes everything in its path. In Richmond, North Yorkshire, police close in on a brutal gang of badger baiters, after a tip-off from a local Rural Watch group leads officers to a car full of suspects and a horrific crime scene

programme.title: Lost and Found in the Lakes
programme.desc: The detectorists climb the fells at Crummock Water in blistering heat, hoping to find a bracelet given to a heartbroken daughter by her late father. programme.title: Tennis: Queen's

Michael finishes on the moonscape Island of Pag, where he helps to make the salty cheese for which the island is famed

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham and Michaela Strachan present the annual series charting the fortunes of British wildlife during the changing of the seasons

programme.title: Pitch Invasion: How the USA Changed Soccer
programme.desc: How superstar players such as Pelé and George Best made America fall in love with soccer in the 1970s, as an entertaining new game emerged, in contrast to English football, which was associated with crumbling stadiums, hooliganism and a grim atmosphere. The New York Cosmos signed Pelé on what was then the biggest contract in sporting history, while Best saw the USA as a chance to reinvent himself and the game. Riding this wave, the NASL boldly predicted global dominance by 1985 - but sadly it was not to be

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.sub-title: River Itchen, Hampshire - Wild Brown Trout
programme.desc: Bob Mortimer and Paul Whitehouse return with a focus on people, places and rivers to which they have personal connections, beginning with Paul taking Bob to a rarely fished spot on the River Itchen in Hampshire, an unspoilt part of this unique chalk stream. Paul also catches up with old friend James Murray, who is leading a campaign to save special rivers such as this for future generations, discussing how they may be able to help reduce the dramatic decline in salmon

Away from Crom, Iolo Williams spends his final night live at Knepp, where rewilding is transforming the landscape

programme.title: Surgeons: At the Edge of Life

programme.title: World's Most Unexplained
programme.desc: Looking at the origin of superstitions surrounding numbers and why so many people are fearful of certain numbers, like the number 13. The fear is so disturbing that hotels skip the 13th floor and airplanes the 13th row

programme.title: World's Most Unexplained
programme.desc: The patterns in fields, which appear overnight and without any warning. What are crop circles, why are they there and what could they possibly signify? Do they come from other worlds?

programme.title: World's Most Unexplained
programme.desc: The Renaissance was a time of great enlightenment throughout Europe, advances were made in both science and the arts. Many paintings have a mysterious common feature, what some would call today a UFO

programme.title: World's Most Unexplained
programme.desc: The mystery surrounding a mass produced series of paintings of crying children, a popular artwork in many houses in the 1980s

programme.title: Discovered by Disaster
programme.desc: Unexpected things revealed by catastrophic disasters, featuring a mythical sculpture unearthed by an Indian tsunami, a 12th-century temple in quake-prone Mexico and a sunken ship in Utah's Great Salt Lake

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson embarks on expeditions through some of Britain's most historic landscapes in search of the richest stories from the nation's past. He begins with a 40-mile walk along the Derwent Valley in the Peak District, where the world's industrial revolution was born. En route he visits the sleepy village of Cromford to find out about the work of entrepreneur Richard Arkwright, and stops off at a 900-year-old stately home that has been used as the location for three Jane Eyre films. Brian Blessed also introduces Tony to the joys - and the significance - of the Cromford canal, before this leg of his journey ends in Derby

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson sets off on a 45-mile hike through the countryside of the Weald in Kent and the Downs of East Sussex to discover the area's rich and surprising Tudor heritage. At Penshurst Place, author Philippa Gregory helps him relish the fate of the Grand Duke of Buckingham at the hands of the young Henry VIII. From there, he travels up what used to be secret paths to Hever Castle and finds out how the monarch's reign brought not only huge social, political and industrial change, but also fame and disaster to the women who caught his eye He ends this leg of his journey in Lewes, where he relives one of the more brutal monastic dissolutions

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and the team arrive in Wood Green, north London, to help members of the public sort the treasures from their trash. Among the items catching the eyes of expert antiques dealers Jan Keyne, David Ford, Ian Towning and Mark Stevens are an Atlas clock, some Crown Derby cutlery and a large amount of gold

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: Rick checks out a rare 1589 manual for war practices and fortifying defences after it is brought into his store

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: A signed script from Seinfeld hits the store, while Chum and Corey go over a barrel for a vintage Donkey Kong arcade machine

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore salvages more items from a recycling centre in Witley, Surrey, with Daniel Heath and Simion Hawtin-Smith helping transforming a floor lamp and a chaise longue

programme.title: Junk & Disorderly: Henry's Wreck-trospective
programme.desc: Compilation of episodes from the show Junk & Disorderly, which features Henry Cole and other restorers buying, fixing, and selling automotive junk

programme.desc: When the Taylor brothers show up, Brandon and Lori have a new civil war on their hands. Brandon and Jesse are forced to team up and help each other to gamble on a once-in-a-lifetime locker

programme.title: Storage Hunters

Brandon and Lori believe the Taylor brothers sabotaged their plans and it comes down to blows for the boys. You don't mess with Brandon and Lori's business

programme.title: Storage Hunters
programme.desc: Buyers haggle over a locker filled with vintage surfing gear. Brandon and Lori get a busted bin that contains a glass case filled with an unidentifiable goo. Later, things get overly intense during the day's final auction

programme.title: Storage Hunters
programme.desc: The buyers try their luck with vault units in San Diego. Brandon and Lori try to stick the Taylor brothers with a questionable vault, but it may just end biting them in the rear. Terrell hits it big with a vault everyone thought was worthless and the Taylor brothers win a vault with a wet-bike

programme.title: Storage Hunters
programme.desc: Brandon and Lori try to win valuable lots at auction. Sean uncovers a bin which has been used for a big prank, while Jesse forges controversial allegiances

programme.title: Storage Hunters
programme.desc: Minds are blown when two of the buyers team up for a major score. Jesse takes a big gamble on some African art

programme.title: Storage Hunters
programme.desc: Brandon and Jesse find themselves in a do-or-die war. A newbie steals a prize unit out from under the regulars, while later Brandon and Lori unearth a five-alarm find

programme.title: Storage Hunters
programme.desc: Buyers bid big on a locker packed with what looks like rare art. Papa Bear spies a profit in a dumpy unit, while expensive looking sky-diving equipment ignites a bidding war

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: Rick gets a visit from The Boss when a Bruce Springsteen tour jacket rocks into the store. Meanwhile, Corey checks out some motorcycle art

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: Rick checks out a bass guitar signed by Led Zeppelin's John Paul Jones and The Who's John Entwistle

programme.title: Junk & Disorderly
programme.desc: Enthusiasts scour the country, challenging themselves to make money out of buying and selling other people's automotive junk and classic collectibles

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Upcycler Sarah Peterson's creativity is tested with a tired set of cane chairs, while robot maker Mark Haig's challenge is to come up with a new use for an old video projector. For his own project, presenter Jay Blades gets crafty with a collection of discarded windows

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Experts explore the disappearance of civilisations throughout time and across the globe, wondering if clues left in their wake point to an otherwordly explanation

programme.title: Craig Charles: UFO Conspiracies
programme.desc: Craig and space journalist Sarah Cruddas consider incidents over the US that it has been suggested are being covered up by the Pentagon. They take on the US Navy UAP videos - the 'Tic-Tac' footage, filmed from the USS Nimitz in 2004 off San-Diego, and the 'Go-Fast' and 'Gimbal' videos filmed between 2014 and 2015 off the East Coast in the Atlantic from the USS Theodore Roosevelt

programme.title: Storage Wars Canada
programme.desc: Father-and-son duo Paul and Bogart go head to head in Claireville, Toronto, with each hoping to claim the bigger profit

programme.title: Storage Wars Canada
programme.sub-title: Big Cow Trouble in Little Ajax
programme.desc: The grand opening of Paul and Bogart's store finally arrives, but their plans suffer a setback when a new buyer shows up on the scene with money to burn

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore salvages more items from a recycling centre in Witley, Surrey, with Daniel Heath and Simion Hawtin-Smith helping transforming a floor lamp and a chaise longue

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and his team head to Wood Green in north London to help people generate cash from their unwanted belongings. Antiques expert Ian Towning doesn't know what to make of a Victorian stereo-viewer and David Ford sees double with a porcelain girl and matching print. Plus, one seller gains a lot of cash for her gold

programme.title: Curse of Skinwalker Ranch
programme.desc: After an explosive experiment aimed at the mysterious Bubble, the team members examine the incredible phenomena they have discovered on Skinwalker Ranch with a number of world renown experts and scientists

programme.title: History's Greatest Mysteries
programme.desc: Actor Laurence Fishburne explores the fate of TWA Flight 800, a Boeing 747-100 that exploded and crashed into the Atlantic Ocean near East Moriches, New York, in July 1996

programme.title: Hazardous History with Henry Winkler
programme.desc: The actor highlights some culinary curiosities, as he revisits some of the most surprising and sometimes alarming food trends from the past. Examples include radioactive energy drinks that once seemed like the next big thing, scandalous snacks made with Olestra and dubious additives in milk and bread. Henry sheds light on how certain foods came to be and the consequences they had

programme.desc: Unforgettable WWE moments, with the spotlight on Mark William Calaway, better known by his ring name of The Undertaker

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.desc: Barry shows up to the auction ready to learn, bringing along a present for a certain hustler. Brandi and Bozek get a firm grip on a smaller unit that raises their spirits, while Ivy treats his son to a birthday locker

programme.desc: San Bernardino is full of surprises, particularly for Rene and Casey, who roll the dice on some sealed totes. Kenny stumbles over some odd-looking car parts, while Lisa shields herself from disaster with an unusually expensive locker

programme.desc: A hairdresser and a builder show that age is just a number. A hip-hop artist meets a musician looking for love off stage. Two 19-year-olds take their first dating steps together and a weightlifter and a bodybuilder weigh up their options

programme.title: Nomadland (2020)
programme.desc: A middle-aged woman is left out of work following the economic collapse of her entire home town. She begins a life outside of conventional society, becoming a modern-day nomad and finding a community of like-minded people adopting a life on the road. Drama, starring Frances McDormand and David Strathairn

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Chef Gordon Ramsay heads to the Greek at the Harbour restaurant in southern California, where owner Makis Mikelatos works 17 hours a day, seven days a week, but is unwilling to let his son Ari take the reins. Gordon sets out to get to the bottom of Makis's reluctance to teach his son, and uncovers a shocking family secret

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer starts his own web page and begins revealing the intimate secrets of everyone in Springfield - but ends up making powerful enemies who imprison him on an island. Featuring the voice of Patrick McGoohan

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge helps a former convict fulfil his artistic ambitions by painting a mural at the school, but he soon clashes with Principal Skinner over the design. With the guest voice of Michael Keaton

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer uncovers a plan to move his beloved Springfield Isotopes baseball team to New Mexico, so he takes desperate measures to keep them in town and goes on hunger strike in protest. With the guest voice of Stacy Keach

programme.title: Sarah Beeny's New Life in the Country
programme.desc: The boys decide to try their hand at wildlife photography. Charlie and Rafferty build their very own deer hide, while Sarah heads to Dumfries House in western Scotland to attend a tea dance in her role as an ambassador for the King's Foundation. programme.desc: Colin Murray and Rachel Riley host the long-running words-and-numbers game with John Thomson joining Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: An entrepreneur leaves his pet in Rebecca's apparently capable hands, but Sam jeopardises her job by letting the dog escape. Comedy, starring Ted Danson and Kirstie Alley

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Sam still believes he can win over Rebecca, especially when she admits there is a song that makes her feel romantic. Cliff trains a new female postal worker, but ends up falling for her. Comedy, starring John Ratzenberger

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Woody turns on the charm while serving at a party for Kelly, the beautiful daughter of a wealthy Cheers executive in a bid to win her affection. American comedy, guest starring Jackie Swanson, with Woody Harrelson and Ted Danson

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Members of the parent-teacher association complain about Debra's outfits, leaving Ray facing the delicate task of persuading her to dress more conservatively in future. Comedy, starring Ray Romano

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ally comes home with a low grade on her report card. Ray is inclined to blame the teacher, but it turns out there is another reason why she is feeling distracted in class. Ray Romano and Patricia Heaton star, with Madylin Sweeten

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray and Frank are pressured by their wives to join Robert in one of his therapy sessions - but the three of them decide to go to the racetrack instead

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Feuding brothers Niles and Frasier decide to find out once and for all which of them has the higher IQ. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and David Hyde Pierce

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: The Seattle shrink takes a dislike to KACL's newest presenter, sharp-tongued radio psychiatrist Dr Nora. Award-winning episode of the comedy series, guest starring Christine Baranski and Piper Laurie

programme.title: Frasier
programme.sub-title: When a Man Loves Two Women
programme.desc: Cassandra Stone, the woman Frasier dated on Valentine's Day, agrees to go out with him again - but the appearance of his former girlfriend Faye, who has been out of the country for a while, complicates matters. Comedy, guest starring Virginia Madsen and Amy Brenneman

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Daphne agrees to marry Donny, but has a vision that suggests he is wrong for her, and tempts fate by turning to Niles for advice on how to proceed

programme.title: American Pickers

programme.title: Channel 4 News Summary
programme.desc: Live from Westminster with the latest news on the PM

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Master mechanic Fuzz Townshend and petrolhead Tim Shaw return to rescue more classic cars for deserving owners. They begin in County Down in Northern Ireland where the 1987 MG Metro 6R4 rally car has turned up in a scrapyard. The family of rally enthusiast Brian put in the call when a serious stroke prevented him from restoring his amazing discovery, and former driver David Gillanders joins in the fun to rescue the car he last drove three decades ago

programme.title: Help! We Bought a Village
programme.desc: More stories of British people rebuilding abandoned historic settlements in Europe. A Scottish lawyer of Italian origin falls in love with the village his forefathers left behind, while Paul and Yip fight to breathe life into their French village

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Mark Chapman in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against words and numbers

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Leah Charles-King helps Karen, a semi-retired lawyer, and Brian, a retired firefighte. They've decided to take a leap and follow their dream of making a permanent move to the mountain villages of Málaga Province in southern Spain

programme.title: Narrow Escapes
programme.desc: Josh, Hayley and their three children leave Lincoln to cruise closer to family in Milton Keynes, racing against time before their new baby arrives. After 25 years at home in Lancashire, fledgling boater Tom finally casts off from his parents, swapping his childhood bedroom for his own narrowboat and a whole new world. In Birmingham, mature student Gen's plumbing springs a surprise

programme.title: Chateau DIY: Australia
programme.desc: Australians gamble everything to restore historic French estates. In Occitanie, DIY duo Janet and Philip start work on restoring their towering 20-metre stairwell, while in the Loire Valley, Suzie races against the clock to transform a 200-year-old disused cattle shed into a showstopping wine bar before nearly 100 guests fly in from Australia for a lavish wedding

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: Sophie and Charlotte help sisters Sandra and Ann from Castle Douglas find Sandra's dream holiday home in the Spanish sun for a maximum budget of 75,000 euros

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: The Dog House
programme.desc: The dedicated team at Woodgreen Pets Charity continue their mission to find homes for abandoned dogs by matchmaking them with hopeful new owners. Glamorous poodle-cross Lola needs to live up to five-year-old Violet's idea of what a dog should look like, while Frankie the Chihuahua plays it cool when he meets best friends Paulette and Jackie. There's a double date in store as Welsh couple Liam and Tabitha meet two very different dogs - Barley the bouncy labradoodle and cuddly Staffie puppy Peanut. However, sparks fly between the two hounds, and it falls to Tabitha to have the final say

programme.title: Tip Toe
programme.desc: Leo and Clive stand alone, face-to-face, in the ultimate confrontation on a night of terror. Meanwhile, Zee reaches out to help George, and they embark on a joyous voyage of discovery. It's a wild night on the streets of Manchester, as violence explodes, souls are laid bare, dangerous games are played and decisions are made that will have terrible consequences for everyone

programme.title: All Of Us Strangers (2023)
programme.desc: Fortysomething screenwriter Adam wrestles with a creative block as he attempts to channel childhood memories into words on his laptop screen. Out of curiosity, he visits his childhood home in Sanderstead and discovers the ghosts of his parents linger at the property. Drama based on Taichi Yamada's novel, starring Andrew Scott, Paul Mescal, Jamie Bell and Claire Foy

programme.title: Emergency
programme.desc: Surgeons Brett and Pete use bone hooks and levers to try pull paratrooper Lee's pelvis out of his spinal canal after a high-speed paragliding accident in France. In south-west London, 86 year-old Veronica comes into St George's Hospital with an open ankle fracture after a car accident but doctors worry there's a vascular injury and she could lose her foot. At St Mary's, Heidi has surgery after a vicious dog attack and 56 year-old plasterer Lee is brought in by air ambulance after a car crash where emergency doctors took over an hour to extract him from the vehicle

programme.title: Gypsy Rose: Life After Lock Up
programme.desc: Gypsy is told that Ryan will be listed as her baby's legal father until a court-ordered paternity test is completed. Social media gossiping doesn't help the situation

programme.title: Gypsy Rose: Life After Lock Up
programme.desc: As Gypsy awaits paternity test results, she plans a 'Babymoon' with Ken to revive their relationship amid social media scrutiny

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Chef Gordon Ramsay heads to the Park's Edge restaurant in Atlanta, Georgia, where a series of spats with the local community are hampering its success. Owners Richard and Jorge's in-fighting has alienated locals, and the duo's suggestion that customer numbers are low because of racism has heightened tensions

programme.title: Falling
programme.desc: Anna feels out of place and struggles to settle back into old routines, seeking guidance from a trusted confidant. David begins to find his feet again, helping out old friends. But as he wrestles with his true feelings, Susan encourages him to take action and face his fears. Romantic drama, starring Keeley Hawes and Paapa Essiedu

programme.title: Falling
programme.desc: As Susan's wedding approaches, David must figure out what it really means to let love in. Anna seeks a fresh start, undeterred by the challenges, and finding support and encouragement from an unlikely source. But as they both embrace change, will their paths bring them together?

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien are in Norfolk to meet upcycler and bike enthusiast Simon. The items they uncover include a filing cabinet, a trunk and a classic bicycle

programme.title: Hollyoaks Omnibus
programme.desc: Dee Dee struggles to hide her emotions as the village gathers for Diane's funeral, Beth continues to seek justice, and a resident's trauma catches up with them. A teenager's grief leads them down a dangerous path, Warren has a surprise for Mercedes, and Beth sets in motion a plan that sends shockwaves through feuding families. Also, Mercedes has a life-altering decision to make

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Mark Chapman in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against words and numbers

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Lilith and Frasier use Carla's six-year-old son as a subject on which to practise their parenting skills. Bar-room comedy, guest starring Jarrett Lennon, with Kelsey Grammer, Bebe Neuwirth and Rhea Perlman

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Rebecca attempts to impress the corporate bigwigs by promoting Norm's painting business, while Sam's sporty girlfriend leaves him exhausted. Comedy, starring Ted Danson

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Frasier and a pregnant Lilith reject civilisation and move to the wilderness to enjoy a more natural way of life, while Woody is forced to take his first holiday since he came to work at the bar

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra invites her divorced parents to Thanksgiving dinner, and Raymond sees something shocking that convinces him they are getting back together. The truth, however, proves far stranger. Robert Culp and Katherine Helmond guest star

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray is annoyed to discover Marie is plying his brother with gifts of scented candles and massage oils. However, his mood changes when he deduces her real motive - to put Robert in a romantic mood, in the hope he will provide her with grandchildren

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Amy's brother Peter turns to Ray for advice on talking to women, while Debra decides to help out by throwing a party and inviting all her single friends

programme.title: Frasier
programme.sub-title: Shut Out in Seattle - Part One
programme.desc: Part one of two. Daphne's engagement to Donny leaves Niles feeling down, so he seeks solace in the arms of a younger woman. Frasier keeps getting his girlfriend's name wrong, Martin's relationship with Bonnie turns sour, and Roz cannot bring herself to break up with Bulldog

programme.title: Frasier
programme.sub-title: Shut Out in Seattle - Part Two
programme.desc: Part two of two. Niles and Roz take extreme action to solve dilemmas in their respective love lives. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer, David Hyde Pierce and Peri Gilpin

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Martin and Niles meet the new woman in Frasier's life - and discover to their surprise that she looks just like his mother. Comedy, guest starring Rita Wilson, alongside Kelsey Grammer, John Mahoney and David Hyde Pierce

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Martin and Frasier inadvertently offer to pay for Daphne's wedding, while Niles sinks into gloom as the marriage approaches and tries to console himself by dating someone new - unaware she is a prostitute. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: The collectors go hog wild at a sprawling ranch in Texas, where Mike Wolfe flips for a VW bus that has been transformed into a hunting lodge on wheels

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Fuzz Townshend and Tim Shaw are in Wolverhampton to take on a 1980 Jaguar XJ-S V12, a great British grand tourer that has sadly fallen into neglect

programme.title: Help! We Bought a Village
programme.desc: A restored village in Italy prepares to host its first guests and the owners of a derelict French hamlet begin renovating their most cherished building. In Puglia, renovations on a quirky hamlet grind to a halt leaving the owners devastated

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray and Rachel Riley host the long-running words-and-numbers game with Mark Chapman joining Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Barbara and Leigh met on the island of Cyprus 20 years ago and even got married there. Now they want to buy a holiday home near Famagusta, close to nightlife, beaches and family oriented towns. Ben Hillman comes up with five options for their £120,000 budget, from apartments with pools to a countryside complex

programme.title: Narrow Escapes
programme.desc: Newcomer Tom officially moves aboard his own boat and begins navigating the uncharted waters of solo living - complete with a gourmet dinner for one. Gen launches her very own podcast, but can she stay afloat balancing broadcast dreams with university deadlines? Josh and Hayley's boat, Darth Vader, powers up for its journey south from Lincoln to Milton Keynes. Adam at the Canal and River Trust is making waves of a different kind, using innovative technology to bring water management on the canals into the modern age

programme.title: Chateau DIY: Australia
programme.desc: In Bordeaux, Deanne and Richard hoist a very expensive 1950s chandelier into place as they race to finish their Brigitte Bardot-inspired bedroom and in Périgueux, Lia and Richard tackle their dilapidated kitchen. In Saint-Émilion, Janey and Steve put in the work to bring their dream en-suite bathroom to life

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: Manjit and Zarrar from Leeds hunt for a holiday home in Almeria with help from father-and-son duo Steve and Shaun Garner and the Rodriguez sisters are in Alicante helping Dave and Paula find a holiday home for their budget of 90,000 euros

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Bake Off: The Professionals
programme.desc: The remaining teams take on the tantalising feast that is pastry. In their first challenge, the teams create 24 savoury laminated tarts and get creative with 24 cream buns. Their showpiece challenge demands precision and flair as they build modern landmarks solely out of biscuit, accompanied by impeccably shaped petit gâteaux, in a towering test of skill, structure and nerve

programme.title: Tip Toe
programme.desc: The war between Leo and Clive reaches a shocking climax. Clive's house becomes a tinderbox of raw emotion as George's secrets spread across town, and even Saul can't stop the onslaught of anger. Zee desperately tries to help, but Leo does exactly the wrong thing as violence explodes on Calico Road and everyone's lives are changed for ever

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Ten-year-old Henry is brought to Queen's Medical Centre in Nottingham after being hit accidentally by a forklift truck driven by his dad. X-rays confirm that both his legs are broken. Nahum and Dean arrive by ambulance following a road traffic accident, and 94-year-old Pearl comes in after badly cutting her arm by trapping it in a lift

programme.title: Building Monaco's Superhomes
programme.desc: Luxury property developer Guy Phoenix embarks on a rollercoaster journey of buying and renovating a £16million Monaco apartment: the biggest investment and financial risk he has ever taken. As Guy makes the first steps towards finding the perfect Monaco apartment to renovate, things go wrong from day one, he quickly discovers that building in one of the most expensive places to live on the planet is anything but easy, as local planning restrictions and impossibly high building costs threaten to turn his lifelong dream of owning a luxury apartment in Monaco into a nightmare. To make matters worse, his £16million apartment doesn't even include a parking space

programme.title: In the Footsteps of Killers
programme.desc: Emilia Fox, David Wilson and Dr Graham Hill investigate the disappearance of Georgina Gharsallah who left her mum's house in March 2018 and was never seen again. The case is now being treated as a murder investigation and an examination of the circumstances of Georgina's disappearance leads the team to a shocking conclusion

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Chef Gordon Ramsay heads to Mike & Nellie's Steakhouse in Oakhurst, New Jersey, where owner Mike Farber is mourning his father Nellie, who was also his business partner. Having been called in by the restaurateur's two daughters, Gordon faces the task of getting him to focus on his business, before tackling the bigger problem of rotting decor and a less-than-appealing menu

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In the Lake District, Paul and Glenda kick off the competition in their static caravan, but eyebrows are raised at his unromantic approach to his relationship. On day two self-confessed gadget geek David and his partner Jane show their competitive side with a confidently presented, well-received meal. Finally outdoorsy twentysomethings Matt and Polly show off their 'good life' approach to cooking with a home-grown feast

programme.title: Dispatches
programme.sub-title: Will My Summer Holiday Be Cancelled?
programme.desc: Kate Quilton investigates the uncertainty over summer holidays, ever since the Iran War sent jet fuel prices soaring and caused thousands of flights to be cancelled. She finds out whether foreign holidays are now out of the question or if the opposite is true, with bargains to be had. She also explores whether holidaying in the UK could be a smarter alternative.

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: The architect meets a couple planning to transform a 1960s ambulance into a campervan, and an artist who has commissioned a giant wooden floating egg to use as a work and living space. He also discovers a novel way of turning a suburban basement in Nottingham into an aquarium, and begins his own project to create a fantasy tree house in the grounds of the Kielder National Park in Northumberland

programme.title: Sunday Brunch
programme.desc: Highlights of the food and chat show, with Tim Lovejoy and Simon Rimmer

programme.title: Sarah Beeny's New Life in the Country
programme.desc: The family goes on a holiday down the Kennet and Avon Canal onboard on a 65-foot barge, something that Sarah has always dreamt of doing. Braving the typically changeable English weather, they take on some tricky locks, fish for their dinner, explore the hills of Avoncliff on bike, venture out on the river to meet some beavers and swim with an urban mermaid

programme.desc: Colin Murray and Rachel Riley host the long-running words-and-numbers game with Mark Chapman joining Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner

Also, Mercedes has a life-altering decision to make

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Rebecca attempts to impress the corporate bigwigs by promoting Norm's painting business, while Sam's sporty girlfriend leaves him exhausted. Comedy, starring Ted Danson

programme.title: Cheers

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra invites her divorced parents to Thanksgiving dinner, and Raymond sees something shocking that convinces him they are getting back together. The truth, however, proves far stranger. Robert Culp and Katherine Helmond guest star

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray is annoyed to discover Marie is plying his brother with gifts of scented candles and massage oils. However, his mood changes when he deduces her real motive - to put Robert in a romantic mood, in the hope he will provide her with grandchildren

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Amy's brother Peter turns to Ray for advice on talking to women, while Debra decides to help out by throwing a party and inviting all her single friends

programme.sub-title: Shut Out in Seattle - Part Two

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Martin and Frasier inadvertently offer to pay for Daphne's wedding, while Niles sinks into gloom as the marriage approaches and tries to console himself by dating someone new - unaware she is a prostitute. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: The collectors go hog wild at a sprawling ranch in Texas, where Mike Wolfe flips for a VW bus that has been transformed into a hunting lodge on wheels

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
