programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Border Force: America's Gatekeepers
programme.desc: A refugee from war-torn Venezuela tells officers how she paid human traffickers to smuggle her through Central America and to the Mexico-United States border. In Laredo, an American couple caught smuggling methamphetamine inside cans of vegetables crumble while being questioned and give officers information about a chain of so-called 'stash houses' where drugs and illegal immigrants are being kept

programme.title: Border Force: America's Gatekeepers
programme.desc: Officers face a harrowing situation as a family from Venezuela pleads with them to enter America. In Progreso, a suspected drug dealer is caught smuggling pills in his underpants

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: The chef reveals the Cornwall that he knows and loves, championing the food, history, music, art and culture of the county. He begins at his family home in Trevose Head on the north coast, where as a child he would go fishing with his father

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick meets a family who make some of the best Gouda cheese in Britain, and uses their creations to make a tortilla dish with caramelised apples and onions. In the fishing village of Mevagissey, he discovers the origins of the sea shanty and the history of the pilchard fishing industry, when shoals of pilchards were so large they would attract killer whales

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick meets chef Tom Adams, who runs Combeshead Farm, a leading field-to-fork restaurant where they grow and produce all of their own food, from sourdough bread to pork pies, as well as rearing a small herd of Mangalitsa pigs, a hairy, old-world breed that produces exquisite hams. Rick tries out a new recipe - pork chops with a sloe berry sauce - before moving on to the village of Rock, where his son Jack holds a clam bake on the shore of the Camel Estuary

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick discovers an unusual superstition and a rare type of Norman castle in Launceston, before meeting musicians Graham Fitkin and Ruth Wall in the far west of Cornwall

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Richard Hammond and James May try mini-cabbing and take the Renault Scenic and the Ford C-Max out for a spin. Jeremy Clarkson gets behind the wheel of the Spyker C8 Spyder, and Lionel Richie puts his motoring skills to the test as the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson puts the Ford GT and the electric/petrol hybrid Toyota Prius through their paces, while Richard Hammond waxes lyrical about Rover's V8 engine. James May scrutinises the Maserati Quattroporte, and actor Martin Clunes is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Comedy actors Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer travel to the picturesque River Usk in south Wales, where Paul's father first taught him to fish as a young child. The duo discuss their respective childhoods and reflect on their own family lives and values. In their cosy cottage accommodation in the heart of the countryside they compare school photographs and Paul serenades Bob with his guitar. Once outside in the picturesque environment, their quarry is the wild brown trout

programme.title: Mediterranean with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon travels through Libya, visiting areas of the country devastated by recent fighting as well as one of the world's best preserved ancient Roman cities. He moves on to Tunisia, where he discovers a mountain fortress and meets a man keeping ancient Berber traditions alive, including making olive oil with the help of camels. Finally, he crosses the sea to Sicily, and discovers how the Mafia are attempting to take advantage of declining population numbers

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Asian Adventure
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King travel around Asia exploring the roots of some of their favourite dishes from the continent. They begin in Hong Kong, where they learn about the versatility of the wok in Cantonese cuisine at a street-food stall, while breakfast reveals a surprising legacy of British rule, before the duo are sent on their way with a traditional Chinese good luck ceremony

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall

In Laredo, an American couple caught smuggling methamphetamine inside cans of vegetables crumble while being questioned and give officers information about a chain of so-called 'stash houses' where drugs and illegal immigrants are being kept

programme.title: Border Force: America's Gatekeepers
programme.desc: Officers face a harrowing situation as a family from Venezuela pleads with them to enter America. In Progreso, a suspected drug dealer is caught smuggling pills in his underpants

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: The chef reveals the Cornwall that he knows and loves, championing the food, history, music, art and culture of the county. He begins at his family home in Trevose Head on the north coast, where as a child he would go fishing with his father

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick meets a family who make some of the best Gouda cheese in Britain, and uses their creations to make a tortilla dish with caramelised apples and onions. In the fishing village of Mevagissey, he discovers the origins of the sea shanty and the history of the pilchard fishing industry, when shoals of pilchards were so large they would attract killer whales

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick meets chef Tom Adams, who runs Combeshead Farm, a leading field-to-fork restaurant where they grow and produce all of their own food, from sourdough bread to pork pies, as well as rearing a small herd of Mangalitsa pigs, a hairy, old-world breed that produces exquisite hams. Rick tries out a new recipe - pork chops with a sloe berry sauce - before moving on to the village of Rock, where his son Jack holds a clam bake on the shore of the Camel Estuary

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick discovers an unusual superstition and a rare type of Norman castle in Launceston, before meeting musicians Graham Fitkin and Ruth Wall in the far west of Cornwall

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Richard Hammond and James May try mini-cabbing and take the Renault Scenic and the Ford C-Max out for a spin. Jeremy Clarkson gets behind the wheel of the Spyker C8 Spyder, and Lionel Richie puts his motoring skills to the test as the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson puts the Ford GT and the electric/petrol hybrid Toyota Prius through their paces, while Richard Hammond waxes lyrical about Rover's V8 engine. James May scrutinises the Maserati Quattroporte, and actor Martin Clunes is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Comedy actors Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer travel to the picturesque River Usk in south Wales, where Paul's father first taught him to fish as a young child. The duo discuss their respective childhoods and reflect on their own family lives and values. In their cosy cottage accommodation in the heart of the countryside they compare school photographs and Paul serenades Bob with his guitar. Once outside in the picturesque environment, their quarry is the wild brown trout

programme.title: Mediterranean with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon travels through Libya, visiting areas of the country devastated by recent fighting as well as one of the world's best preserved ancient Roman cities. He moves on to Tunisia, where he discovers a mountain fortress and meets a man keeping ancient Berber traditions alive, including making olive oil with the help of camels. Finally, he crosses the sea to Sicily, and discovers how the Mafia are attempting to take advantage of declining population numbers

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Asian Adventure
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King travel around Asia exploring the roots of some of their favourite dishes from the continent. They begin in Hong Kong, where they learn about the versatility of the wok in Cantonese cuisine at a street-food stall, while breakfast reveals a surprising legacy of British rule, before the duo are sent on their way with a traditional Chinese good luck ceremony

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: In a special episode filmed in Ghana, Diane goes in search of Ric to persuade him to return home. She finds him working in his brother's clinic, the only available source of medical care for the local community, which is in desperate need of funding. An outraged Ric demands she take the first flight back to the UK, declaring that he's needed where he is. But Diane is not convinced his reasons for staying are entirely altruistic, and investigates unresolved conflicts in his family concerning the death of his father. As she prepares to return home, an emergency involving a pregnant woman brings their personal and professional dilemmas to a head. Adrian Edmondson guest stars

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Part two of two. The refurbished ED opens for the first time since the fire and the new facilities are put to the test when a traffic accident occurs on a nearby dual carriageway, while a pipe explosion causes a cloud of poisonous gas to engulf Holby. The staff are stretched to their limits despite the efforts of new paediatric doctor Tom Kent (Oliver Coleman), and as tempers fray, a patient lashes out at Dylan. Guest starring Marshall Lancaster (Ashes to Ashes) and Colin Buchanan (Dalziel & Pascoe)

programme.sub-title: From Audrey Nash to the Widow Dilhooley

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: Double-length episode, originally shown at Christmas 1986. The Resistance plan to steal a plane to fly the airmen back to Britain. A parade of antique vehicles is arranged as a diversion, while Rene works on an unusual means of launching the aircraft

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: As the Gibraltar locals reel from the murder of shipping agent Gordon Fletcher, Brian and Gerry - last seen being carried off in a shipping container - find themselves stranded on the side of a Spanish mountain, and now face the problem of how to get back to the rest of the team. As Sandra attempts to unravel secrets stretching back over three decades and link what has now become three murders, she still has doubts about the innocence of charming entrepreneur Harry Truman - but that doesn't stop her joining him for lunch on his luxury yacht. Conclusion of a two-part story, with regulars Amanda Redman, Alun Armstrong, Dennis Waterman and Denis Lawson joined by Vincent Regan and George Irving

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Following his recovery from a life-threatening head injury, Leo's first case involves the shattered body of a woman found at the bottom of a block of derelict flats. A post-mortem indicates suicide, but the investigation is reopened when the pathologist uncovers evidence that the victim was shot before being thrown off the roof, and his suspicions are aroused when DI Neill seems reluctant to pursue the possibility that the deceased was an informant for an undercover police officer

programme.title: New Zealand: Earth's Mythical Islands
programme.desc: Sam Neill narrates the story of some of New Zealand's wildest places, home to hyper-intelligent parrots, snails with teeth, and constellations of glow-worms. In these often inhospitable locations, any creature hoping to survive must resort to radical techniques, none more so than on the South Island, which lies toward Antarctica and is home to the rapidly rising peaks of the Southern Alps

programme.title: Wild Scandinavia
programme.desc: A look at the beauty of the region's forests and waterways through the seasons, with ingenious ravens working together to outsmart golden eagles to get a meal, while osprey swoop down to catch fish from lakes shaped by beavers. In the spring, a wandering male lynx leaves his scent on a twisted pine, hoping to attract a mate, while a mother bear emerges from hibernation and tries to teach her playful cubs to climb

programme.title: Wild Scandinavia
programme.desc: The dramatic extremes of the Nordic territories - volcanic Iceland, the high tundra and Arctic Svalbard - where polar bears, arctic foxes and musk ox must all survive in a world that ranges from total darkness to midnight sun. For some, winter is about resilience and scraping through until the summer feasts, but for others it's the summer heat that brings the greatest danger.

programme.title: New Zealand: Earth's Mythical Islands
programme.desc: Sam Neill narrates a documentary examining the wildlife of the island chain, which has been left to its own devices for 80 million years - with surprising consequences. The opening edition features New Zealand's weirdest and most ancient species, including dinosaur-like reptiles, insects that have become giants, and flightless birds that behave like badgers, while the people who settled developed their own unique culture, and all must battle the natural forces that shape the island nation

programme.title: New Zealand: Earth's Mythical Islands

programme.title: Expedition with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve and the team battle through the jungle into the heart of Indonesian Borneo. They are on their way to meet experts who have discovered ancient cave art thought to be among the oldest on the planet. But they have also heard rumours that the mountain range contains further unmapped caves - and Steve wants to be the first to track them down, and whatever is hidden within

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: The naturalist continues to explore the secrets behind evolution, this time focusing on the animal kingdom's bizarre hybrids, including the 'pizzly bear', a cross between a polar bear and a grizzly, which is thought to have come into being as a result of global warming. He also covers the killer bee brought into existence due to the transfer of African bees to South America

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: Footage of animals that have unusual means of finding their way around. Dung beetles are able to navigate by the sky, using the position of the sun, moon and stars to determine which is the right direction to go in, while pigeons display remarkable powers of memory, able to recall complex travel routes and follow manmade roads and hedgerows

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?
programme.desc: Bob and Thelma throw a surprise party to cheer up Terry - but the blindfolded guest of honour begins to insult old friends, unaware they are listening to his every word

programme.title: Wild Scandinavia
programme.desc: A look at the beauty of the region's forests and waterways through the seasons, with ingenious ravens working together to outsmart golden eagles to get a meal, while osprey swoop down to catch fish from lakes shaped by beavers. In the spring, a wandering male lynx leaves his scent on a twisted pine, hoping to attract a mate, while a mother bear emerges from hibernation and tries to teach her playful cubs to climb

programme.title: Wild Scandinavia
programme.desc: The dramatic extremes of the Nordic territories - volcanic Iceland, the high tundra and Arctic Svalbard - where polar bears, arctic foxes and musk ox must all survive in a world that ranges from total darkness to midnight sun. For some, winter is about resilience and scraping through until the summer feasts, but for others it's the summer heat that brings the greatest danger.

programme.title: New Zealand: Earth's Mythical Islands
programme.desc: Sam Neill narrates a documentary examining the wildlife of the island chain, which has been left to its own devices for 80 million years - with surprising consequences. The opening edition features New Zealand's weirdest and most ancient species, including dinosaur-like reptiles, insects that have become giants, and flightless birds that behave like badgers, while the people who settled developed their own unique culture, and all must battle the natural forces that shape the island nation

programme.title: New Zealand: Earth's Mythical Islands
programme.desc: Sam Neill narrates the story of some of New Zealand's wildest places, home to hyper-intelligent parrots, snails with teeth, and constellations of glow-worms. In these often inhospitable locations, any creature hoping to survive must resort to radical techniques, none more so than on the South Island, which lies toward Antarctica and is home to the rapidly rising peaks of the Southern Alps

programme.title: Expedition with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve and the team battle through the jungle into the heart of Indonesian Borneo. They are on their way to meet experts who have discovered ancient cave art thought to be among the oldest on the planet. But they have also heard rumours that the mountain range contains further unmapped caves - and Steve wants to be the first to track them down, and whatever is hidden within

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: The naturalist continues to explore the secrets behind evolution, this time focusing on the animal kingdom's bizarre hybrids, including the 'pizzly bear', a cross between a polar bear and a grizzly, which is thought to have come into being as a result of global warming. He also covers the killer bee brought into existence due to the transfer of African bees to South America

programme.desc: As Lee Mack brings the popular sitcom to an end, a chance to see some of the unintentionally comic moments during filming that ended up on the cutting-room floor

programme.title: dinnerladies diaries
programme.desc: Behind the scenes of the British sitcom, featuring interviews with the cast as they reminisce about the show on its 20th anniversary

programme.desc: Tyler falls into a deep depression after his girlfriend leaves him, spending every hour painting gloomy pictures and starving himself. programme.title: Men Behaving Badly
programme.desc: Dermot offers his body and Gary fixes up a burglar alarm in forlorn efforts to impress Deborah

programme.title: Harry Enfield and Chums
programme.desc: Mr Cholmondley-Warner offers his idiosyncratic views on madness, while Stan and Pam flash their cash in Spain. Kevin refuses to go to bed, Tim gets engaged to a royal and Alf Git meets an old flame at a funeral with explosive results

programme.title: Men Behaving Badly
programme.desc: Dermot and Gary get serious over a chess contest in which the victor gains an evening of pleasure. Laddish comedy, starring Martin Clunes and Leslie Ash

programme.title: Inside No 9
programme.desc: Joe and Chas have been together for nine years, a long time to be truly in love with someone. But how long does it take to properly say goodbye? Darkly comic tale, written by and starring Steve Pemberton and Reece Shearsmith, with a guest appearance from Sheila Reid

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: Peggy becomes a Yellowcoat, but her happiness is spoilt when Alec Foster arrives with bad news. Last-ever episode of the hit comedy, starring Su Pollard and Ruth Madoc

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel Fitzpatrick treats a Persian cat that has been hit by a car, requiring treatments involving a skin graft and putting its crushed bones back together. Meanwhile, a rescue dog has a cancerous tumour in his jaw removed, and a young staffie-cross needs treatment for a developmental elbow disease

programme.title: Who Killed Jenni Rivera?
programme.desc: Rocked by threats in her inner circle, Jenni made major life changes before the crash. Police learn two people on the plane had alleged ties to the cartel

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: Edina starts a TV company - and pops up on daytime television as a result - while daughter Saffy tries for a job with New Labour. Sozzled Sixties throwback Patsy seeks medical help to disguise the ravages of middle age, while former Blue Peter presenter Katy Grin struggles to get back on telly

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: Saffy's choice of landscape gardener gives Eddie something to gawp at, and she vows to get her claws into the hunk. However, Patsy soon turns up with a shock revelation - it seems the green-fingered guru is actually a card-carrying member of the aristocracy, sending Eddy's imagination into overdrive

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: Patsy jumps at the opportunity to relive the modelling days of her distant past when she joins a fashion shoot in Paris - but soon tires of the fresh-faced mannequins who haunt her at every turn. Meanwhile, Edina hopes the French capital is just the place for a bonding session with Saffy - if only she can get her to change her frumpy blouse. Sacha Distel guest stars

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: MasterChef Australia
programme.desc: The home cooks are presented with a shipping container-sized mystery box - the biggest ever in MasterChef history - marking the start of Big Week

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles helps Sue Roberts, who was left with a run-down cottage near Dumfries, when her partner Barry died of a heart attack on Christmas Day 2003. She found herself suffering from depression with two children to look after, so Nick enlists a team of helpers to fix up the property and assist the family with getting their lives back on track

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: In the heart of the West Midlands, Nick Knowles and local volunteers help a Dudley family whose three children have a genetic learning disorder. The Whitehouses tried to add an extension to their house, but after living in a building site for a year it had become too much. The build has to be completed in just nine days, and plummeting sub-zero temperatures make the task even more daunting

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles heads to Duxford in Cambridgeshire, where he enlists the help of local tradespeople to assist Paul and Sarah Behan. The couple had started on an extension to their small bungalow when the project had to be abandoned due to their son Joshua being diagnosed with a rare and aggressive form of liver cancer. The team puts in an impressive effort, but with only nine days to finish the job the challenge is extensive

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Martin and Shirlie Kemp are on the road with Margie Cooper and James Braxton, and have their eyes on a collection of vintage comics and an umbrella stand

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Children's TV presenters Radzi Chinyanganya and Karim Zeroual travel around Wales looking for item to take auction, with the help of experts Paul Martin and Catherine Southon. Radzi decides to quite literally go for gold as he gambles more than half his budget on the precious metal, while Karim hopes there is money to be made from a 1950s stone duck

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Cameras catch up with the work of the pioneering veterinary practice, where some of the hardest-to-cure pets receive cutting-edge treatment. When one-year-old shepherd doodle Raven started limping at only a few months old, his local vet diagnosed double hip dysplasia. Now, owners Alan and Jane find themselves at Fitzpatricks to discuss possible total hip replacement for both back legs. Also receiving help are three-month-old kitten Fury, who has a suspected tibia injury sustained during boisterous play and two-year-old police dog Trigger who has degenerative lumbosacral stenosis, causing pain in his lower back

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: One-year-old golden retriever Bertie developed a dislocated hip joint and curvature of his spine and is in a lot of pain. Noel has to find an implant to treat Bertie's arthritis and prevent his hip from dislocating and work out a way to manage the effect of his spinal deformity over time. Trusty Tibetan terrier, nine-year-old Sunny recently received a total knee replacement using an implant system called a rotating hinge. However, before a second procedure is staged a flare up of the auto-immune inflammatory arthritis in Sunny's carpi casts a dark cloud over his future once again

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles helps Sue Roberts, who was left with a run-down cottage near Dumfries, when her partner Barry died of a heart attack on Christmas Day 2003. She found herself suffering from depression with two children to look after, so Nick enlists a team of helpers to fix up the property and assist the family with getting their lives back on track

programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs' Sake
programme.desc: Pete travels to Northern Ireland to pick up three stray dogs and bring them back to Essex. He also meets a Shar Pei who is in constant pain and may need surgery

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: Emergency services deal with the consequences of gun crime, racing to the scene when a man is shot outside a mosque

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: The Hazardous Area Response Team is called to help defuse a bomb found in Wakefield, and to rescue a BMX rider who fell and fractured his spine in woods near Leeds

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: In the Home Counties, TV doctor and GP Amir Khan takes on presenter and talk-show host Trisha Goddard in a competition to make the most money from antiques, assisted by experts Tim Medhurst and Izzie Balmer

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Children's TV presenters Radzi Chinyanganya and Karim Zeroual travel around Wales looking for item to take auction, with the help of experts Paul Martin and Catherine Southon. Radzi decides to quite literally go for gold as he gambles more than half his budget on the precious metal, while Karim hopes there is money to be made from a 1950s stone duck

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: Edina goes on a detox diet in another desperate attempt to get thin, but since this means cutting out the beloved Bolly it's likely to be just as tough on everyone else. Meanwhile, Saffy bumps into an old school friend with thespian pretensions and Patsy hits on the hare-brained notion of trying life without her famous fringe. Featuring Marianne Faithfull as God

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: Saffy's autobiographical play is about to open at a fringe theatre and her father Justin arrives to provide moral support while Eddie, fearing public humiliation, lurks in the wings scheming with Bubble to ruin the show. Starring Jennifer Saunders, Joanna Lumley, Julia Sawalha, Christopher Ryan, Mo Gaffney, Naoko Mori and Miles Western

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: As Edina's PR company collapses round her ears thanks to Bubble, Saffy forces her to attend a Menopause Anonymous meeting, where she can share the experiences of other women and learn to love her hormone patches. The 'change' appears to be having an alarming effect on Patsy, however, who is turning into something of a domestic goddess. With guest stars Twiggy, Ruby Wax and Dale Winton

programme.title: MasterChef Australia

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles heads to Maidstone, Kent, to meet former Royal Engineer and paratrooper Mo Morris. After 20 years in the service, he was given a medical discharge due to damage to his knees, and has been left struggling to walk unaided ever since. At present, his home is unsuited to his needs, putting pressure on his relatives as they try to help as best they can. Determined to give him back his independence, the DIY SOS team rallies the local troops, including tradespeople and neighbours, to work together and adapt Mo's house, with designer Gabrielle Blackman on hand to make sure everything goes to plan

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team come to the aid of a couple in Halifax, West Yorkshire, whose eight-year-old son is paralysed and can only access two rooms in their house. The family home is in need of a major overhaul but with only nine days to complete the job, the gang needs help - and that's where local tradesmen and other members of the community come in, turning up in force to bring designer Gabrielle Blackman's ideas to life

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Since his wife died shortly after giving birth to their sixth child, Paul Makings has been valiantly holding the family together as they grieve. But he's facing even more problems - living in a two-bedroom house he has started to extend, and forced to sleep on the sofa. Nick Knowles and the team are joined by more than 100 locals in Misterton, Nottinghamshire, as they take on their biggest home build ever. However, they have to battle against a blizzard to complete the job in nine days

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: In the Home Counties, TV doctor and GP Amir Khan takes on presenter and talk-show host Trisha Goddard in a competition to make the most money from antiques, assisted by experts Tim Medhurst and Izzie Balmer

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Made in Chelsea's Sam Thompson takes to the Cotswolds B-roads with his best friend, Pete Wicks from The Only Way is Essex. Under the supervision of experts Margie Cooper and Raj Bisram, these reality TV star rivals go head-to-head as they swap on-screen antics for auction-worthy antiques

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Toy poodle Monty was jumped on by a larger dog, damaging his hip joint. Monty is so small that very few surgeons can perform a complex total hip replacement on such a miniature scale, so the family are hoping that Noel can help. Another two-year-old, 75kg Caucasian shepherd dog Odin, has a condition where malformed vertebrae in his neck are putting pressure on his spinal cord, causing pain and impairing the nerve signals to his back legs

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A 'puppy special' recalling the amazing stories of some of the youngest, fluffiest animals for whom Noel has provided life-saving treatment since The Supervet began. They include three-month-old springer spaniel Poppy, who was treated in 2016 when she presented with some of the worst front leg deformities Noel had ever seen. In 2018, nine-month-old Jack Russell Bella was admitted as an emergency after being injured in a freak accident, falling from a carrier on a bike and badly fracturing her pelvis

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles heads to Maidstone, Kent, to meet former Royal Engineer and paratrooper Mo Morris. After 20 years in the service, he was given a medical discharge due to damage to his knees, and has been left struggling to walk unaided ever since. At present, his home is unsuited to his needs, putting pressure on his relatives as they try to help as best they can. Determined to give him back his independence, the DIY SOS team rallies the local troops, including tradespeople and neighbours, to work together and adapt Mo's house, with designer Gabrielle Blackman on hand to make sure everything goes to plan

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team come to the aid of a couple in Halifax, West Yorkshire, whose eight-year-old son is paralysed and can only access two rooms in their house. The family home is in need of a major overhaul but with only nine days to complete the job, the gang needs help - and that's where local tradesmen and other members of the community come in, turning up in force to bring designer Gabrielle Blackman's ideas to life

programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs' Sake
programme.desc: Strictly Come Dancing and TOWIE star Pete Wicks immerses himself in the heart-warming and emotional world of dog care at Basildon's Dogs Trust rehoming centre. Pete returns to Dogs Trust, facing a suitcase of abandoned pups on day one and falling hard for Theo, a gentle Italian Mastiff with a serious heart condition.

programme.title: Hashtag Homicide
programme.desc: In April 2016, London police officer Gordon Semple was killed during a Grindr meetup, with texts and digital footprints guiding detectives through the case

programme.title: Hashtag Homicide
programme.sub-title: The Murder of Ana Abulaban and Rayburn Cardenas Barron
programme.desc: In October 2021, Ana Abulaban and Rayburn Cardenas Barron were killed by Ana's estranged husband, TikTok influencer Ali Abulaban

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Consumer journalists Nikki Fox and Matt Allwright hunt for antiques in Kent alongside expert guides Natasha Raskin Sharp and David Harper. As they seek out items to sell at auction, the rookie antiques dealers find the time to learn about William Morris's manifesto to save ancient buildings, and discover the connection between Kentish ragstone and the early Victorian dinosaur hunters

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Made in Chelsea's Sam Thompson takes to the Cotswolds B-roads with his best friend, Pete Wicks from The Only Way is Essex. Under the supervision of experts Margie Cooper and Raj Bisram, these reality TV star rivals go head-to-head as they swap on-screen antics for auction-worthy antiques

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: Celebrity PR Edina works from home and struggles to cope after Saffy's departure. Matters come to a head when client Emma Bunton arrives for a meeting, and is horrified at the state of the house, but luckily Saffy turns up and lets slip a big secret that might just render her mother speechless. Comedy, starring Jennifer Saunders and Joanna Lumley

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: Saffy's pregnancy sends her mum off the deep end, bringing nightmares about becoming a grandmother, her torture compounded by the taunting midwife. Luckily, Eddie's new celebrity book club started with Patsy takes her mind off her troubles, as Mariella Frostrup promises to bring her famous friends

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: Patsy resents Saffy's new close relationship with her mother, but is soothed by an encounter with guest star Minnie Driver. Edina's delight at the prospect of having a mixed-race grandchild is topped by her installation of a panic room, complete with champagne and a surveillance system

programme.desc: Today's service challenge is in Melbourne's Spotswood, a huge park filled with street food outlets. Every team must create two dishes, surf and turf, each to be sold for $10

Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles heads to Maidstone, Kent, to meet former Royal Engineer and paratrooper Mo Morris. After 20 years in the service, he was given a medical discharge due to damage to his knees, and has been left struggling to walk unaided ever since. At present, his home is unsuited to his needs, putting pressure on his relatives as they try to help as best they can. Determined to give him back his independence, the DIY SOS team rallies the local troops, including tradespeople and neighbours, to work together and adapt Mo's house, with designer Gabrielle Blackman on hand to make sure everything goes to plan

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team come to the aid of a couple in Halifax, West Yorkshire, whose eight-year-old son is paralysed and can only access two rooms in their house. The family home is in need of a major overhaul but with only nine days to complete the job, the gang needs help - and that's where local tradesmen and other members of the community come in, turning up in force to bring designer Gabrielle Blackman's ideas to life

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Since his wife died shortly after giving birth to their sixth child, Paul Makings has been valiantly holding the family together as they grieve. But he's facing even more problems - living in a two-bedroom house he has started to extend, and forced to sleep on the sofa. Nick Knowles and the team are joined by more than 100 locals in Misterton, Nottinghamshire, as they take on their biggest home build ever. However, they have to battle against a blizzard to complete the job in nine days

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: In the Home Counties, TV doctor and GP Amir Khan takes on presenter and talk-show host Trisha Goddard in a competition to make the most money from antiques, assisted by experts Tim Medhurst and Izzie Balmer

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Made in Chelsea's Sam Thompson takes to the Cotswolds B-roads with his best friend, Pete Wicks from The Only Way is Essex. Under the supervision of experts Margie Cooper and Raj Bisram, these reality TV star rivals go head-to-head as they swap on-screen antics for auction-worthy antiques

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Toy poodle Monty was jumped on by a larger dog, damaging his hip joint. Monty is so small that very few surgeons can perform a complex total hip replacement on such a miniature scale, so the family are hoping that Noel can help. Another two-year-old, 75kg Caucasian shepherd dog Odin, has a condition where malformed vertebrae in his neck are putting pressure on his spinal cord, causing pain and impairing the nerve signals to his back legs

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A 'puppy special' recalling the amazing stories of some of the youngest, fluffiest animals for whom Noel has provided life-saving treatment since The Supervet began. They include three-month-old springer spaniel Poppy, who was treated in 2016 when she presented with some of the worst front leg deformities Noel had ever seen. In 2018, nine-month-old Jack Russell Bella was admitted as an emergency after being injured in a freak accident, falling from a carrier on a bike and badly fracturing her pelvis

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles heads to Maidstone, Kent, to meet former Royal Engineer and paratrooper Mo Morris. After 20 years in the service, he was given a medical discharge due to damage to his knees, and has been left struggling to walk unaided ever since. At present, his home is unsuited to his needs, putting pressure on his relatives as they try to help as best they can. Determined to give him back his independence, the DIY SOS team rallies the local troops, including tradespeople and neighbours, to work together and adapt Mo's house, with designer Gabrielle Blackman on hand to make sure everything goes to plan

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team come to the aid of a couple in Halifax, West Yorkshire, whose eight-year-old son is paralysed and can only access two rooms in their house. The family home is in need of a major overhaul but with only nine days to complete the job, the gang needs help - and that's where local tradesmen and other members of the community come in, turning up in force to bring designer Gabrielle Blackman's ideas to life

programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs' Sake
programme.desc: Strictly Come Dancing and TOWIE star Pete Wicks immerses himself in the heart-warming and emotional world of dog care at Basildon's Dogs Trust rehoming centre. Pete returns to Dogs Trust, facing a suitcase of abandoned pups on day one and falling hard for Theo, a gentle Italian Mastiff with a serious heart condition.

programme.title: Hashtag Homicide
programme.desc: In April 2016, London police officer Gordon Semple was killed during a Grindr meetup, with texts and digital footprints guiding detectives through the case

programme.title: Hashtag Homicide
programme.sub-title: The Murder of Ana Abulaban and Rayburn Cardenas Barron
programme.desc: In October 2021, Ana Abulaban and Rayburn Cardenas Barron were killed by Ana's estranged husband, TikTok influencer Ali Abulaban

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: In the Home Counties, TV doctor and GP Amir Khan takes on presenter and talk-show host Trisha Goddard in a competition to make the most money from antiques, assisted by experts Tim Medhurst and Izzie Balmer

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Broadcasters Mark Radcliffe and Edith Bowman put down their mics and battle it out to pick up some profit-making collectables and curios. Antiques experts Paul Martin and Natasha Raskin Sharp are along for the ride as they roar around the roads of the Midlands in an iconic DeLorean and a classy-looking Rover

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: Celebrity PR Edina works from home and struggles to cope after Saffy's departure. Matters come to a head when client Emma Bunton arrives for a meeting, and is horrified at the state of the house, but luckily Saffy turns up and lets slip a big secret that might just render her mother speechless. Comedy, starring Jennifer Saunders and Joanna Lumley

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: Saffy's pregnancy sends her mum off the deep end, bringing nightmares about becoming a grandmother, her torture compounded by the taunting midwife. Luckily, Eddie's new celebrity book club started with Patsy takes her mind off her troubles, as Mariella Frostrup promises to bring her famous friends

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: Patsy resents Saffy's new close relationship with her mother, but is soothed by an encounter with guest star Minnie Driver. Edina's delight at the prospect of having a mixed-race grandchild is topped by her installation of a panic room, complete with champagne and a surveillance system

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: MasterChef Australia
programme.desc: Today's service challenge is in Melbourne's Spotswood, a huge park filled with street food outlets. Every team must create two dishes, surf and turf, each to be sold for $10

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Dave and Jack head to East Yorkshire to pick up a dusty Alfa Romeo and a 1930s Jowett. But Dave's more taken with a Carry On-style, bum-wobbling exercise machine

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Derek is heading to Scotland to collect a 'special' Bentley that's been created by a very talented amateur

programme.title: Flipping Bangers
programme.desc: Car enthusiasts Gus and Will want to buy a VW Beetle. They locate a guy selling a project car, but fear it's almost too good to be true at less than £1,000

programme.title: Flipping Bangers
programme.desc: Will Trickett and Gus Gregory find an orange Reliant Kitten that an engineer has given up on - the body is detached from the chassis, it doesn't run and most of the bits it needs are in boxes in the boot. However, the guys are sure there's money to be made from it

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Speed with Guy Martin
programme.desc: The motorcycle racer and lorry mechanic attempts, with the aid of slipstreaming, to break the British record for outright speed on a bicycle - 110mph. Guy recruits a team comprising a truck racer, a bike builder and a design engineer to prepare for the feat, and undergoes a training regime with double Olympic cycling gold medallist Laura Trott before heading to Pendine Sands on the shores of Carmarthen Bay for his challenge

programme.title: Speed with Guy Martin
programme.desc: For his second adrenaline-fuelled challenge, the racer tries to set the world record for riding a motorcycle on water. With the help of a Cambridge professor and a team of marine engineers, he attempts the feat at Bala Lake in Snowdonia, with extra fittings on his bike that should enable it to skim across the surface. Crashing is inevitable, so Guy endures a rigorous training schedule to prepare himself for the task

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Derek heads to Suffolk to collect two Bentleys with a majestic pedigree; Cynthia a stunning 1963 S3 and Harry, a 1965 Continental

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Dave heads to West Yorkshire to collect a rare 1926 Dodge bus. Derek gets his hands on a rare Vauxhall Victor VX 90 police car that is expected to sell for more than a few coppers

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Acclaimed chronicle of the Second World War, beginning with an account of Hitler's ascent to power at a time when the Germans were stricken by humiliating military defeat, runaway inflation and economic depression. The Nazi leader's promises of regained national pride and prosperity struck a chord with millions of ordinary citizens, and he was elected on a tidal wave of popular support. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: Secret Nazi Bases
programme.desc: One of the largest Nazi bunkers ever constructed, outside the French town of Watten, a missile facility with storage for 108 missiles, a liquid oxygen factory and two launch pads

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team examine artefacts brought to the Old Royal Naval College in Greenwich. Objects include the personal effects of Queen Mary's bodyguard, a beloved Victorian toilet, and a painting of a female spy dating back to World War One that leads experts to discover an even more valuable work of art

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team search for hidden treasure at the Old Royal Naval College at Greenwich, where items include one of the rarest sets of medals ever brought to a Roadshow and a Lalique glass figure found by a chambermaid under a bed. The host also learns the truth about how a wait for a bus is often ended by three turning up at once

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Derek's search for the unusual lands him a 1947 Lea-Francis that's half made of wood. Paul heads for the countryside to pick up a Morris one tonne truck

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Dave and Jack head to East Yorkshire to pick up a dusty Alfa Romeo and a 1930s Jowett. But Dave's more taken with a Carry On-style, bum-wobbling exercise machine

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie helps rescue a runaway narrowboat on the scenic Macclesfield Canal and cruises through autumn scenery toward Congleton to see a shipwreck up for sale

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Fallen trees block Robbie's journey on the Peak Forest Canal. Later, he meets TV host Terry Christian, whose dad once worked a local waterway

programme.title: Operation Gold Rush with Dan Snow
programme.desc: Dan Snow, Felicity Aston and Dr Kevin Fong build a wooden boat, following original designs for the vessels used during the Gold Rush. This vessel must carry them across hundreds of miles of treacherous lakes and rivers, including the infamous One Mile Rapids, a narrow canyon of white water and jagged rocks

programme.desc: Derek's search for the unusual lands him a 1947 Lea-Francis that's half made of wood. Paul heads for the countryside to pick up a Morris one tonne truck

programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien search for more items to fix up and sell on, visiting a Norman castle, restoring a Fiat 500 and making a table from part of Southend pier

programme.title: Speed with Guy Martin
programme.desc: Speed freak Guy Martin embarks on a mission to fly using muscle power alone, by building the world's fastest human-powered aircraft. He heads to Southampton University where, in 1961, Derek Piggot became the first man to fly under his own power. Guy teams up with a group of engineers from the same university to build a plane that Guy will ride like a bicycle - will he manage to fly into the record books?

programme.title: Speed with Guy Martin
programme.desc: The racing fan sets out to break the record for the world's fastest gravity-powered sled, taking expert advice from Winter Olympic gold medallist Amy Williams. Guy experiences his first taste of going blisteringly fast on ice at the famous Cresta Run in the Swiss ski resort of St Moritz, before a crack team of engineers helps him build a prototype toboggan. Then it's on to Andorra in the Pyrenees, where he hopes to steer himself into the record books on the new speed ski slope Pista Riberal

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Dave heads to Bolton to pick up a classic Italian sports car - a Lancia Fulvia 1.3 Sport styled by Milan coachbuilder Zagato

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Derek rubs shoulders with pop music royalty when he picks up a Morgan sports car, while a Second World War ambulance arrives at Thornton le Dale

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The months following prime minister Neville Chamberlain's declaration of war against Germany, in which the only casualties on the home front were those who had to endure the blackouts. Elsewhere in Europe, the Nazi war machine cut a swathe of destruction. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: Secret Nazi Bases
programme.sub-title: Dag Bromberg: A City in the Forest
programme.desc: As the Red Army approached a forest in Poland toward the end of the war, they stumbled upon a massive complex containing more than a thousand structures across 25sqkm

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and her team of experts travel to Beverley Minster in East Yorkshire, where the items up for valuation include a pair of Victorian paintings that were given as payment for a gambling debt and a medieval ring dug up on a farm. They also reveal the surprisingly eventful past of a car rescued from a pig farm

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The team pays a second visit to Beverley Minster, East Yorkshire, where the objects up for scrutiny include a pair of medical leech jars, a ring containing a highly valuable lock of hair, and a distinctive sideboard featuring some very well-hidden drawers. Presented by Fiona Bruce

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Derek Mathewson picks up a lovingly restored 1925 Calthorpe motorbike that he thinks is more of a work of art than anything ever produced by Picasso

programme.desc: The team buy a confusing 1984 French Matra Murena and send it to Citroen specialists. Next, they take on a Bond classic - no, not that one - a 1977 Arctic Wetbike!

programme.title: Speed with Guy Martin
programme.desc: The motorbike racer takes part in four more speed-based challenges and gets his hands dirty in unique engineering projects to discover what makes things go fast. In the first programme, he finds out how far it's possible to travel on a bicycle during 24 hours of non-stop pedalling. With the help of bike experts Miles Kingsbury and Mike Burrows he builds a tandem, and pairs up with his friend, endurance expert Jason Miles, to see how far they can push their bodies. In the build-up to the final record attempt at the historic Goodwood Motor Circuit, Guy also learns about the science of cooking for athletes with nutritionist Nigel Mitchell, who instructs him to lose five kilos and recommends a new diet regimen

programme.desc: Dave Mathewson gets wind of a possible free lunch at the officers' mess when he heads off to collect a stylish Swedish Volvo P1800S from an RAF base in the Midlands

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The months following prime minister Neville Chamberlain's declaration of war against Germany, in which the only casualties on the home front were those who had to endure the blackouts. Elsewhere in Europe, the Nazi war machine cut a swathe of destruction. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: Secret Nazi Bases
programme.sub-title: Dag Bromberg: A City in the Forest
programme.desc: As the Red Army approached a forest in Poland toward the end of the war, they stumbled upon a massive complex containing more than a thousand structures across 25sqkm

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and her team of experts travel to Beverley Minster in East Yorkshire, where the items up for valuation include a pair of Victorian paintings that were given as payment for a gambling debt and a medieval ring dug up on a farm. They also reveal the surprisingly eventful past of a car rescued from a pig farm

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: It's Robbie Cumming's toughest trip yet as he battles through water weed on the Bradley Arm and gets stuck in silt on the Ridgacre Branch

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Paul hits the road to collect an old navy ambulance that served its time as a holiday camper

programme.title: Bangers & Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The team buy a confusing 1984 French Matra Murena and send it to Citroen specialists. Next, they take on a Bond classic - no, not that one - a 1977 Arctic Wetbike!

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Derek Mathewson picks up a lovingly restored 1925 Calthorpe motorbike that he thinks is more of a work of art than anything ever produced by Picasso

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Dave Mathewson gets wind of a possible free lunch at the officers' mess when he heads off to collect a stylish Swedish Volvo P1800S from an RAF base in the Midlands

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: The Mathewsons take in a 1982 Audi Quattro and a Sunbeam Alpine. Plus, Derek takes delivery of a beautiful green Renault van, previously used as a bread van in Paris

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole restores two quad bikes and a stylish sun lounger, while Simon O'Brien rescues a holed canoe and turns an old oak beam into a spice rack

programme.title: Speed with Guy Martin
programme.desc: Speed freak Guy Martin embarks on a mission to fly using muscle power alone, by building the world's fastest human-powered aircraft. He heads to Southampton University where, in 1961, Derek Piggot became the first man to fly under his own power. Guy teams up with a group of engineers from the same university to build a plane that Guy will ride like a bicycle - will he manage to fly into the record books?

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Speed with Guy Martin
programme.desc: The motorbike racer takes part in four more speed-based challenges and gets his hands dirty in unique engineering projects to discover what makes things go fast. In the first programme, he finds out how far it's possible to travel on a bicycle during 24 hours of non-stop pedalling. With the help of bike experts Miles Kingsbury and Mike Burrows he builds a tandem, and pairs up with his friend, endurance expert Jason Miles, to see how far they can push their bodies. In the build-up to the final record attempt at the historic Goodwood Motor Circuit, Guy also learns about the science of cooking for athletes with nutritionist Nigel Mitchell, who instructs him to lose five kilos and recommends a new diet regimen

programme.title: Speed with Guy Martin
programme.desc: The motorcycle racer heads to Colorado where he hopes to win one of America's oldest races - the Pikes Peak International Hill Climb. In this annual contest, known as the Race to the Clouds, riders compete against the clock on a gruelling mountainside track that finishes more than 13,000ft above sea level. To learn the skills required in hill-climb events, Guy visits the birthplace of the sport - Shelsley Walsh in Worcestershire. Racing up the course in an F1-style car, he realises sheer horsepower and driving precision are vital, but he must also take a high-altitude fitness test in a hypoxic chamber to prepare for the challenge ahead

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: The owners of Paramount Hotel in Nottingham push Alex Polizzi to the edge and she comes close to walking out for the first time ever. Mary and Milad have ploughed their life savings into the business, remortgaged their home, borrowed from relatives and rented rooms for tiny amounts to undesirable guests in an attempt to keep their heads above water. Will Alex have the power to change their fortunes?

programme.title: Good Witch
programme.desc: Sam successfully operates on Adam, but not without complications. Joy dreams that Abigail and Donovan break up, and against Cassie's advice, tries to stop it from happening

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Early morning chat show presented by Lorraine Kelly, with celebrity interviews, entertainment news, cooking tips, fashion features and musical guests.

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: The boxer talks about his life inside and outside the ring. From growing up as a triplet to when he became the first Irishman to win an Olympic Gold medal in boxing in 1992

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: Three singletons try to impress rugby player Jakob Williams in Cardiff, who then goes on a date with the person whose taste in clothes he liked best

programme.title: In for a Penny
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts the game show from locations around the UK, setting members of the public unusual challenges for a chance to win anything from the price of their petrol, to £1000 in the big finale game. This week, Stephen is in Nottingham

programme.title: In for a Penny
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts the game show from locations around the UK, setting members of the public unusual challenges for a chance to win anything from the price of their petrol, to £1000 in the big finale game. This week, Stephen is in Bristol

programme.title: What Would Your Kid Do?
programme.desc: Children are filmed in a school specially rigged with hidden cameras. In the studio, host Jason Manford challenges parents to predict what their child will do in each situation, providing an insight into the developing minds of five to seven year-olds. The family with the most correct predictions goes through to the final. And the kids choose the prizes

programme.title: Yes Chef
programme.desc: Culinary challenge hosted by Sheree Murphy in which chef Ryan Simpson puts three home cooks through a series of challenges, including how to make the perfect steak tartare

programme.title: The Six O'Clock Show
programme.desc: Chat show featuring celebrity interviews, food from leading chefs and guidance on a wide range of topics, from consumer affairs to the latest trends, with viewers invited to contribute with pictures, comments, stories and gossip

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: The Instant Gardener
programme.desc: Helen Skelton and designer Danny Clarke have just one day to transform a grey and windswept roof terrace in Basingstoke, Hampshire into a cosy and colourful outdoor room for owner Aimee

programme.title: Peter Andre's 60 Minute Makeover
programme.desc: Alice Lee enlists the help of Peter and interior designer Leah Hughes to revamp her mother's home in Warlingham, Surrey, after she suffered an upsetting burglary. They tackle the living room and conservatory and turn the spaces into fun, feminine and chic rooms

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: The documentary going behind the scenes of the London airport returns for a fifth season. Inside the terminal, seasoned pro Demi dishes out deals for delayed passengers, while outside on the airfield, Ian is on the runway as it goes into lockdown with a full armed police presence. For the first time this series, viewers will also see the work of the Border Force's canine drugs detection team - Rachel and her dog Cole, who in his career has intercepted more than £1million worth of class-A contraband. Narrated by Julie Walters

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Staff at Heathrow airport contend with drunken passengers, drugs and drones, while Sue and Demi deal with an extra load of passengers after a closure at Gatwick airport. Elsewhere, Border Force officer Bob returns to the job, working to protect Britain's biggest border

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: A colossal American football stadium in Texas, that features the world's largest retractable roof and is able to seat over 100,000 spectators with no restricted viewpoints

programme.title: Hotel Benidorm: Fun-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: With the Benidorm Plaza full to bursting, and housekeeping pushed to their limits, the clock is ticking as bride-to-be Hannah and her hen party are keen to check into their rooms to get the party started. Meanwhile, restaurant manager Pepe is challenged by new hotel manager Angel to revamp his weekly Gala Dinner

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: Real-life small-claims cases on issues affecting family life, presided over by outspoken former New York judge Judy Sheindlin

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants - Dick from Brighton, Jenny from Surrey, Evie from Corby and Matthew from Altrincham - pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.desc: Dating show, which follows single parents who have been nominated by their grown-up children for a second chance at love

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi heads to Crawley in West Sussex to help out a family-run concern just five miles away from Gatwick. Despite its proximity to the busy airport, the Waterhall Country House Hotel is struggling and owners Anne and Andy Marsh desperately need a change in fortunes if it is to become profitable. Can Alex set them on the way to success?

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport

programme.desc: Alison is invited on a lavish trip to a luxurious hotel with Liam and soon discovers that he is using her as cover to spy on Amir Joshi and his family. programme.desc: At Battersea Dogs and Cats Home, Alison meets an excitable spaniel who's been living with a very serious knee issue. She also encounters a frustrated crossbreed desperate to use his nose, and helps teach sign language to a gorgeous Staffy who's had a lifetime of ear problems

programme.title: Call the Cleaners
programme.desc: Shaun and his team are called out by a landlady who found her property in a very bad state, with mess over the floors, graffiti on the walls and doors pulled off their hinges. Huw is surprised to see his daughter Margot back, and Alice kicks Huw out of the house when she discovers that he has lied to her

programme.title: Code of Silence
